🚨Upozorňujeme na zranitelnost v 7‑Zip, CVE-2026-14266. Jedná se chybu vznikající při zpracování XZ komprimovaných (chunked) dat během dekomprese, kdy dochází k přetečení vyhrazeného paměťového prostoru na haldě v důsledku nesprávného zpracování vstupních dat. Útočník může tuto zranitelnost zneužít vytvořením speciálně upraveného archivního souboru nebo dodáním škodlivého XZ obsahu, přičemž po jeho otevření uživatelem dojde ke spuštění libovolného kódu v kontextu běžícího procesu se stejnými oprávněními jako má přihlášený uživatel. Zneužití vyžaduje interakci uživatele, typicky otevření škodlivého archivu nebo návštěvu stránky, která takový soubor doručí, přičemž nejsou vyžadována žádná předchozí oprávnění ani autentizace. Zranitelnost postihuje všechny verze 7‑Zip starší než 26.02.  📌Doporučujeme aktualizovat na verzi 26.02 nebo novější.

NVIDIA might have open-sourced one of the most important ai projects right now.  everyone is building skills, and we are also pulling in skills other people wrote and downloading them straight off GitHub.  the skill is not just text. it bundles instructions and real executable code, and your agent runs that code with the same access you have.  so a skill you grabbed to save ten minutes can read your environment variables, lift your api keys, and quietly send them somewhere. recent research found roughly 1 in 4 public skills carry a vulnerability, and a smaller slice are outright malicious.  that is the gap SkillSpector closes. it is a security scanner that answers one question before you install anything: is this skill safe to run.  you point it at a skill, and a local folder, a single skill .md file, a GitHub link, or a zip all work.  it then runs two passes over the code. a fast static pass flags risky patterns like credential harvesting, data leaks, and prompt injection, and checks the dependencies against live cve data.  an optional second pass uses an llm to read intent and clear out false positives.  at the end you get one risk score from 0 to 100 and a plain verdict that reads as safe, caution, or do not install.  it is open source under Apache 2.0 and scans skills for Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Gemini.  worth a run before you trust the next skill you find online.  link to the GitHub repo: github.com/NVIDIA/SkillSpect…

🛡️   A newly disclosed zero-day flaw in AnyDesk, tracked as CVE-2026-15682, allows local attackers to crash affected installations by abusing a core support feature, raising fresh concerns for organizations relying on the remote desktop tool for IT support and access management.  The vulnerability resides in AnyDesk's Send Support Information feature, which is designed to help users share diagnostic data with support teams during troubleshooting sessions.  By creating a junction, a type of filesystem reparse point that redirects file operations, an attacker can trick the AnyDesk service into writing arbitrary files outside their intended location.  #cybersecuritynews

六把锁，同一个钥匙孔：六家顶级 AI 助手栽在一个符号链接老洞上 整条街的门锁都是同一款，小偷只用配一把钥匙，一晚上就能开完整条街。这个月安全圈出的事就是这个路数：一个 1970 年代就有的老毛病，一次撂倒了六家顶级 AI 编程助手。  1由头  GhostApproval 到底是个啥：六家一起踩了同一块旧地板 名单拉出来，基本是这个行当的全家福。  7 月 8 日，安全公司 Wiz 发了一份披露，给这个漏洞模式起名 GhostApproval，一次点了六家：Amazon Q Developer、Claude Code、Augment、Cursor、Google Antigravity、Windsurf。你手上正在用的那个，多半也在里头。  说白了，这些工具干活都有个规矩：真要往你硬盘上写文件、真要动点危险的东西，得先弹个窗问你一句「我要写 project_settings.json，行不行？」你点了同意，它才动手。这个环节行话叫人在环（Human-in-the-Loop），整套系统的最后一道关。理论上，AI 想干啥坏事，你都能在这一步喊停。  GhostApproval 没碰这道关本身，它骗的是弹窗上那行字。  为什么值得看：单看一家，这就是个普通漏洞，补了就完。可六家同时中招，说明栽的是一整套被行业当标配抄来抄去的设计。 2手法  符号链接这招骗术：给你看一个门牌号，把信塞进另一家的信箱 这招老得很，Unix 时代就有的经典头疼。  符号链接（symlink），你可以把它想成一张便利贴，作用就是指路。你打开它，系统顺着它指的方向，去开真正那个文件。这东西本身没毛病，是文件系统几十年的常规功能。  攻击者的花招搭在这上头。往一个恶意代码仓库里放一个文件，起名叫 project_settings.json，听着人畜无害，像个正经配置。它其实是张便利贴，指向你电脑上沙箱外面的 ~/.ssh/authorized_keys。这个文件管免密登录，谁的公钥写进去，谁就能直接登你这台机器。  你把仓库拉下来让 AI 助手干活，它要写 project_settings.json，弹窗照规矩问你。你一看，项目配置嘛，点了同意。写入顺着便利贴走，落在了 ~/.ssh 里。  3橡皮图章  人在环为啥拦不住：你审的是那行字，写的却是另一个地方 这道防线一行代码都没被破解，照样失守了。  人在环这道设计，本意特别正：AI 不可全信，那就让人来把最后一道关。可它有个没说出口的前提，你得看得见 AI 到底要干啥。弹窗上写「写 project_settings.json」，你脑子里默认的就是往项目里写个配置。你审的是这行字，你同意的也是这行字。  问题就出在，这行字和真正发生的事，是两码事。你审了个无害路径，系统执行的却是被便利贴重定向后的沙箱外写入。你按下的那个同意键，认的只是弹窗上那个假门牌。  核心用 Wiz 原文的定性：这把一次沙箱绕过，变成了一次知情同意绕过；「人在环」这道安全网，沦为一个橡皮图章。审批对话框显示的路径，和真正发生的写入，是两回事。 橡皮图章这东西，我 2013 年见过实物。那年我还在外包公司，给华南一家城商行做网银改造，行方所有变更走纸质签批，一式三份、六个章。4 月 17 日，一条把某档利率小数点挪了一位的配置，六个章全盖齐，照样上了生产，回滚搞到凌晨四点，行里食堂给加班的人煮了两锅面，行内后来管这类事故就叫「4·17」。你说那六个章有哪个是乱盖的？没有。每个人都核了自己该核的那一栏，只是没有一栏管这个数对不对。我的结论就是那晚落下的：章管的是谁负责，不管对不对。弹窗上那个同意按钮，跟那六个章是同一个东西。  4照进现实  凯文·凯利《失控》里的老账：整齐划一的田，一种病就能团灭 这种整整齐齐一起倒的阵型，《失控》里早写过。  凯文·凯利在《失控》里反复讲一件事：活的系统靠多样性活命。他举的是单一栽培（monoculture），一整片田种的全是基因一模一样的作物，看着高产、整齐、好管理，软肋也在这儿：来一种对得上路的病原体，整片田一起完，零星死几株都算好结局。爱尔兰那场马铃薯饥荒就是这么来的，全国的土豆是同一个品系，一种病菌，就够了。  ◎打个比方：你别看这六家 AI 助手是六个牌子、六拨人马、各写各的代码，可它们认定用户点没点头的办法是同一份：把路径显示给人看，人点了就算数。这就跟六户人家门锁样式不同，可锁芯是同一批货一个道理。 这就是单一栽培的脆弱：六家各自看，都在抄行业最佳实践；可正因为抄的是同一份，它们也共享了同一个盲区，没人去把符号链接的真实目标解开来看一眼。本该六道防线互相兜底，落地成了六个一模一样的橡皮图章。  5别急着骂  这锅不能全甩给工具：厂商响应分了岔，争议也没停 厂商这边的反应，信息量比骂声大。  反应分了岔（细节见下表）：AWS、Cursor、Google 都已部署修复，Augment 和 Windsurf 至今未处理。此外，这到底算不算漏洞、在不在自家威胁模型内，业界一直有争议：有厂商最初就判定这不在自己的威胁模型里，后来才补了个警告。  厂商响应时间 / 编号 Amazon Q Developer已修复language server 1.69.0，2026-05-27 · CVE-2026-12958 Cursor已修复v3.0，2026-06-05 · CVE-2026-50549 Google Antigravity已部署修复，正评估是否发 CVE2026-05-22 Augment至今未处理— Windsurf至今未处理— 那这锅到底该谁背？我的看法是，别急着把「AI 工具不靠谱」这句话焊在某一家脑门上。符号链接跟随是个几十年前就有的老漏洞类，Unix 时代的经典头疼，从来没真正消失过。这些 AI 工具不过是把同一套文件系统原语包了一层新皮，顺手把旧伤口一起继承了下来。真要怪，得怪大家都懒得去解符号链接，这份默契跨了半个世纪。  厂商响应与 CVE 编号均据 2026 年 7 月 8 日的公开披露与报道；「这算不算漏洞、在不在威胁模型内」尚有争议，本文不对任何具名厂商作未经证实的定性。技术细节以各厂商官方公告为准。 6这对你意味着什么  下回你点「同意」之前，先问一句：这弹窗给我看的，是真的吗 厂商的补丁管厂商的事，你手上的习惯得自己改。  你要是天天用这些 AI 助手写代码，GhostApproval 给的教训特朴素：审批弹窗上那行字，不等于真正会发生的事。遇到来路不明的仓库，别拉下来就让 AI 一路点同意干到底，尤其是涉及写文件的操作。人在环这道关，只有在你看得见真相的时候才算数。  看见真相这件事，土办法早就有。我父亲在八卦岭开五金店那些年，柜台常年备一瓶丙酮。2001 年他进过一批「全新」继电器，一个开维修部的老客户当场摸出放大镜看丝印，字体不对，是打磨翻新的，父亲按行规赔了双倍。打那以后进货先拿丙酮擦一下丝印，翻新的一擦就露馅。丝印是印给你看的，丙酮擦出来的才是底子。审批弹窗上那行路径就是丝印，这回六家都没备丙酮：把符号链接解开、看真实写入目标这一步，谁都没做。  至于往后怎么走，我把注押在这儿：这轮补丁挡得住 project_settings.json，挡不住下一个换皮的老原语。硬链接、路径大小写，哪家现在防得住，我没逐个验证过，但这类原语在同一个抽屉里躺了几十年。一年之内，弹窗显示的和实际发生的对不上号这种事，还会再上一轮新闻，主角换个名字而已。这话放这儿，明年今天可以回来对账。  六家共用同一套按显示路径放行的审批设计，被一个 1970 年代就有的老洞一次通杀。截至 2026 年 7 月 8 日披露：三家已部署修复，Augment 和 Windsurf 仍未处理。

Here are 7 FREE cybersecurity courses you've never heard of that you start this weekend 👩🏻‍💻👀  Everyone shares the same handful of platforms, so I went looking for the underrated ones. These are all free to start, hands-on, and beginner friendly:  1. 🔒 Root Me: root-me.org/?lang=en Hundreds of free challenges across web, network, forensics, and crypto.   2. 🛡️ AttackIQ Academy: academy.attackiq.com/ Free courses on MITRE ATT&CK, detection engineering, and purple teaming.  3. 🔍 CyberDefenders BlueYard: cyberdefenders.org/blueteam-… Free DFIR, threat hunting, and malware analysis labs that run right in your browser.  4. 🕵🏻‍♀️ Blue Team Labs Online: blueteamlabs.online/ A gamified platform covering incident response and threat hunting.   5. 🎓 BreachLab: breachlab.org/ Comprehensive offensive security training from Linux basics to red team operations.  6. 💥 Metasploit Unleashed: offsec.com/metasploit-unleas… The free ethical hacking course from OffSec, and one of the best intros to exploitation.  7. 🧪 SEED Labs: seedsecuritylabs.org/ Free university grade security labs on buffer overflows, SQL injection, and cryptography.  There's a LOT of cybersecurity training out there, so hopefully this list gives you something new to start with   Drop any other underrated training in the comments, I would love to see what the community is using!  🌟 Repost this to share with your network 🌟  #cybersecurity #cybersecuritytraining #cybersecurityjobs

AIを「安全なモデル」に仕上げるだけで十分なのでしょうか。  Google DeepMindが示したのは、将来、企業内部で使われるAIが望ましくない目標を持ち、監視を欺こうとする可能性まで想定した、「サイバーセキュリティ型のAI制御ロードマップ」です。  本稿は、現在のAIが実際に企業へ反旗を翻していると報告するものではありません。アラインメントという第一の防衛線が破れた最悪の場合を想定し、AIを潜在的な内部脅威として扱います。  既存のMITRE ATT&CKを拡張した「TRAIT&R」により、コードの改変、情報流出、研究妨害、複数のAIによる分散型攻撃などを整理しています。  そのうえで、AIの「監視回避能力」と「攻撃実行能力」に応じて、思考・行動の監視、隔離、権限管理、リアルタイムの遮断、緊急停止など15の対策を段階的に強化する枠組みを提案しています。  重要なのは、AIの目標を正しく保つことだけに安全性を委ねず、たとえ望ましくない目標を持っていても、外側のシステムで行動を検出し、阻止し、被害から復旧する「第二の防衛線」を、フロンティアAIラボが公式のロードマップとして打ち出した点です。  ただし、これは実証済みの万能策ではなく、今後の実装と検証を必要とする設計図です。  GDM AI Control Roadmap. Mary Phuong, Erik Jenner, Laurent Simon, Lewis Ho, Rohin Shah, Sebastian Farquhar, Scott Coull. Arxiv:2607.13087

Build projects that prove you can do the job.  Top 3 projects to build:  1. SOC investigation with written incident report  Investigate real attacks on a VM, analyze logs, and write a report: timeline, IOCs, MITRE ATT&CK mapping, recommendations. The report is the product.  2. Third-party vendor risk assessment  Onboard a fictional SaaS vendor. Send a security questionnaire, review a SOC 2, score the risks, and write a risk acceptance memo with remediation asks.  Assessment workbook + 1-page exec summary.  3. Identity lifecycle (JML) audit  Set up Entra ID or Okta, simulate joiner/mover/leaver scenarios, audit for orphaned accounts, excessive permissions, and missing offboarding. Document gaps against a framework. Shows you understand access risk, not just tools.  Build these. Document them. Talk about them.

sample_tweets.text: Regarding the recent HFM USDJPY incident, it is clear that the traders who actively tried to exploit the campaign have suffered the most damage.  In particular, clients under certain IBs that heavily promoted the campaign seem to have borne the brunt of the impact.  While HFM likely intended the reward to be a modest bonus for regular trading, some participants exploited loopholes in the system—either by trading with excessively large lots or using AI-generated EAs for high-frequency trading. It remains unclear whether this officially constitutes "fraudulent activity," but I can understand the counterargument that the broker should not have designed such a flawed campaign structure. In that regard, the broker is highly culpable.  However, the online communities managed by those who encouraged users to deviate from their normal trading practices just to "farm" this campaign are reportedly in total chaos right now. Those promoters seriously need to reflect on their actions.  From a professional standpoint, if an IB truly values their clients, they should never recommend such a campaign as a "campaign-farming" opportunity.  A trader's role is to trade, not to farm promotions. Recommending this solely based on the "lucrative benefits" without properly explaining the risks of getting flagged for exploit trading is a severe breach of trust.

President Trump said tonight the CCP's operations obtained 220 million voter records. The threat comes from combining the data: weaponizing voter roles, health care records, and security clearance applications can build an entire profile of a US citizen.   This can be used for fraudulent voter registration, identity theft, targeting US citizens for recruitment by China.

Threadguy reveals why Bitcoin is already private enough if you know what you are doing.  Total privacy is a double-edged sword. With networks like Zcash or XMR, if an exploit happens and new tokens are secretly minted, nobody would ever know. The supply is completely hidden, making it impossible to verify.  The Airmass thesis gets it right: if you are diligent with understanding your keys and how the network works, Bitcoin is private enough without taking on that massive blind spot.  Selling privacy coins as a better Bitcoin is a rough pitch. They are just an alternative. But despite the technical flaws of completely private coins, the broader concept of privacy is still the most underpriced narrative of the next ten years.

BIG FAT RED FLAG for anyone who thinks smart contract audits are the only security layer that matters.  This week attackers compromised Injective Labs' GitHub account and pushed a malicious npm package straight into their dependency chain, built to steal wallet keys from anyone who installed it.  Not a contract exploit. Not a bridge hack. A supply-chain attack on the tools developers trust by default.  The good part: Injective's team caught and reverted the malicious package in 49 minutes.  The part that should worry you:  > The attack vector wasn't the blockchain, it was the developer's own toolchain > npm installs run arbitrary code with zero onchain visibility > 49 minutes was fast only because someone was actively watching  Most teams don't have that response time. Most users have no way to know if a package they installed last week was ever compromised.  If you've pulled any Injective-related package in the last two weeks, have you rotated your keys yet?
An intruding exploit, indeed.  Fruitless, however.

The team is working with law enforcement, cybersecurity firms and industry partners to trace stolen funds.

Domain: 6  - Hash: 38  - IP: 9  - File: 9  - Url: 1  💽Software: Docker, QEMU, Android, MariaDB, nginx, Minecraft, TeamSpeak, FiveM, Busybox, ThinkPHP, ...  🔢Algorithms: poly1305, sha512, xor, chacha20-poly1305, curve25519, sha256, ed25519, pbkdf2, base64, sha1, chacha20  🔠Functions: system, toggle_obf, add_entry_plaintext, dga_generate_domain, exploit_engine_init, sendmmsg, attack_init, select  🗂️Win API: inet_addr  📜Programming Languages: golang, python, php  💻Platforms: arm, mips, ppc64, riscv64, m68k, x86  #threatreport: TuxBot v3 Evolution has been identified as a previously undocumented modular IoT botnet framework that prominently utilizes large language model (LLM) assistance in its code development. Despite its innovative approach, this reliance on AI has resulted in various functional shortcomings within the malware. The botnet is capable of targeting devices through Telnet brute-forcing, employing 1,496 credential pairs, and utilizing exploit code for over 30 different IoT device families. Communication with the command-and-control (C2) server is secured via an encrypted TCP channel, where a SHA512 domain generation algorithm (DGA) helps manage connectivity.  The bot agent is constructed in C, with cross-compilation for multiple architectures, while the C2 server is developed in Go and includes a panel for DDoS-for-hire capabilities. Despite promising functionality, many features within the analyzed version of TuxBot are not operational, including critical exploit mechanisms and various scanning capabilities. For instance, the exploitation system encountered multiple bugs due to LLM-generated code, which led to some functions being ineffectively implemented or completely non-functional.  The framework organizes its C2 architecture across multiple communication layers, employing several channels such as IRC and HTTP, though significant portions of this infrastructure are rendered non-functional by errors in the coding. Key vulnerabilities were also identified in the malware's design, including an XOR encryption key mismatch that compromised critical strings necessary for operation.  TuxBot's exploitation capabilities encompass three categories with varying degrees of readiness. While 16 hard-coded exploit functions exist, none are actively utilized in the current build. However, modular exploit file structures allow for integration of up to 13 CVEs once functional. Additional features are planned for exploitation; however, they are currently not operational due to coding flaws.  Persistence mechanisms embed the malware deeply within infected systems, using various techniques familiar in IoT malware, such as service disguise and file manipulation. Self-defense strategies are also incorporated to detect and evade common security measures, including checks against known analysis tools and virtual environments.

sample_tweets.text: Hosted generously by The Fed, the day explored the past, present and future of Manchester's Jewish community, the nature of pivotal leadership, and the individuals and organisations helping to shape the community's future. We were privileged to hear from an outstanding group of speakers and panellists- among them communal leaders and alumni, culminating in a deeply moving testimony from Yoni Finlay (survivor of the Yom Kippur attack on Heaton Park Synagogue) on resilience, second chances and the responsibility we have to one another. Participants came from Manchester, Leeds, Glasgow, Brighton as well as the London area.  A huge thank you to The Fed for hosting us and to Raphi Bloom, whose vision, organisation and facilitation were instrumental in making the day such a success alongside Rachelle Lazarus and Candice Mendes da Costa and the alumni steering group.  Closing the event, Debbie Fox, Interim CEO of the Jewish Leadership Council, reflected on the extraordinary potential of our growing alumni network to strengthen collaboration across the community.  Drawing on Yoni's powerful metaphor of Holocaust survivors sharing a single blanket between many people, she reminded us that leadership is not about holding resources, opportunities or influence for ourselves, but about ensuring that the "blanket" is shared as widely as possible.  And finally, a special mention to those who made the train journey back to London, with most arriving just in time for kick-off of England v Argentina… unfortunately for all that effort, the result didn't quite go our way.

Armenian Petroglyphs from 9,000-3,000 BC  Cave Writing from Lake Sevan. Before hieroglyphic writing was developed between 900 and 700 BC, the inhabitants of the Armenian Highlands expressed themselves by carving and painting on the rocks.  These three pictograms were completed between the 8th and 4th millennia BC (from 9,000 to 3,000 BC). According to archaeologists, the drawings are associated with Neolithic cultures, especially in the higher mountain regions, including Aragats, Armaghan, and the Lake Sevan basin.  Armenian language and its roots  The Indo-European language group is one of the most widely spoken language families in the world. Around 3000 BC, the proto-Indo-European languages were probably closely connected to each other. But by 2000 BC, Greek and two extinct languages, Hittite and Sanskrit, already differed one from another quite significantly.  Although changes in grammar and definitions have taken place, vocabulary analyses show that people of ancient Indo-European culture were farmers, knew how to handle iron, and had developed cattle breeding.  Recent discoveries also reveal common ties with the Kurgan people, who lived in the steppes to the west of the Urals.  Samples of Armenian hieroglyphs were revealed in excavations in Karmir Blur, where ceramics, bronze cups, and cylindrical shells were unearthed. This type of writing was used by the people living in the Ararat valley long before the establishment of the Kingdom of Van.  Famous English poet Lord Byron studied the Armenian language on the island of St. Lazarus in the monastery of the Mekhitarists in Venice, Italy, from 1816 to 1823. He wrote: “It is a rich language, however, and would amply repay any one the trouble of learning it”. In a note of April 11, 1818, he wrote: “You will not neglect my Armenians.”  By PeopleOfAr Source: University of Minnesota

Breaking News: Self-styled Ambazonia separatist commander "General Sagat," identified as Tandu Lazarus, was killed this Thursday, July 16, during a military operation in Ekona, South West region, Cameroon News Agency reported, citing military sources.  Sagat, who led a group known as the Fako Mountain Lions, was reportedly killed after troops acting on intelligence about the movement of suspected separatist fighters carried out an operation in the area.   The Fako Mountain Lions is a separatist militia active in the Fako Division and is considered loyal to Samuel Ikome Sako, a diaspora-based separatist leader who heads a faction of the Ambazonia Interim Government.  Troops reportedly intercepted two men travelling on a motorcycle before carrying out an ambush. General Sagat was killed during the operation, while a second suspected fighter was injured and arrested.  The military said it recovered two mobile phones and an undisclosed amount of cash from them.   #Cameroon #NorthWest #SouthWest #Ekona #Ambazonia #Conflict #MMINews

July 15, 2026 jesusandscience.org/2026/07/…  Dear Friends,  This week, five men prayed with me according to the pattern of Luke 18:13 & Romans 10:9.  A young man, mid 20s, in the UK, wrote,  “Hi James! I struggle to be convinced by Jesus’ resurrection, could we organise a meeting or could you help me in any way?”  He has an education in movie production, but since COVID, that field has cratered. So he is presently working in a primary school. A very friendly young man who listened carefully and readily accepted the word of truth. He is being mentored by his associate now using “Growing in Christ”.  Acts 16:14 “One of those listening was a woman from the city of Thyatira named Lydia, a dealer in purple cloth. She was a worshiper of God, and the Lord opened her heart to respond to Paul’s message.” On the advice of wise counsel, over the next few weeks, I will be winding down most of the one-on-one appointments I had scheduled with seekers. I was getting overwhelmed by too many one-on-one appointments. Though I never mention it, in many of those scheduled meeting times, the seeker doesn’t show up at our agreed time, and I wait for up to 15 minutes. So it can really slice up an evening. I have declined many other activities over the years in order to attend these one-on-one meetings, so when they do not show up, it is troubling. I truly enjoy speaking one-on-one to share the gospel. It is my greatest joy in life. But I am getting overwhelmed by the level of interest.  Brandon set up a Webinar-based system, and I will hold two sessions per week, during which I can meet with several people at a time. It is not as personal as the one-on-one, but it is the only way to ensure my physical survival. This way, I can meet with people in small groups of 3 to 7, while the technology itself would permit meetings of hundreds. Also, if a person or two do not show up, the meeting can still ensue. So far, the meetings are held twice per week: on Saturdays at noon and again at 8 pm, so people can access them easily from anywhere in the world without work or sleep-time conflicts. We have held two such sessions, and while there are bugs to work out and refinements that we will make over the next few weeks, it is a far more efficient course. Additionally, others have signed up to serve as mentors, which was desperately needed.  The people below attended the webinar.  A Canadian videographer in his 40s wrote, in part:  “Hi Dr. Tour, Thank you for making time to meet with people who are genuinely searching. I’ve been wrestling with the physical resurrection of Jesus for quite a while. I’m hoping to better understand why you believe the resurrection is the best explanation and to ask a few honest questions if time allows.”  After the webinar, he wrote,  “Thanks so much for putting this together. I just finished your Zoom lecture today, and I really appreciate you taking the time to share your insight. I’ll be straight with you—I’ve been looking for a solid male mentor for over four years now. Hearing you talk about this program as a genuine labour of love really resonated with me. I’m genuinely motivated to learn and grow, and I’m excited about the possibility of working with someone experienced who can help guide me forward, both personally and spiritually. Thanks again to you and everyone who made this happen. Looking forward to connecting.”  Rice Broocks will assign the mentor for this Canadian seeker.  Hebrews 10:25 “not forsaking our own assembling together, as is the habit of some, but encouraging one another; and all the more as you see the day drawing near.”  Next was a man from Romania with many questions. He wrote, in part:  “Why is it that Jesus’ corpse was not simply revived, like Lazarus? Why the challenge of delayed recognition? Mary Magdalene thinks he is the gardener until he speaks her name: Mary (John 20:14 16). The two disciples on the road to Emmaus walk with him for a long time, but their eyes were kept from recognizing him until the breaking of bread (Luke 24:16, 30 31). The disciples by the Sea of Tiberias do not immediately know it is Jesus on the shore (John 21:4). I do have a hard time processing these evidences. Kind regards, _______.”  We met on the webinar. Again, it suffered from a lack of one-on-one interactions, during which I could not see his face, though he could see my face. After I presented the gospel, he acknowledged that he had prayed with me. He also asked this same question during the question-and-answer time. I cited the psychological study titled “Recognizing Familiar Faces Out of Context” (doi.org/10.1177/030100662098… ). Where unexpected faces are often not recognized, as would be Jesus after rising from the dead. And I mentioned other possible reasons: disfigurement from the scourging and crucifixion, as we know he still bore the wounds on his hands and side, and possibly the Lord needed to open the eyes of their hearts to recognize him.   Rice Broocks will assign the mentor for this Romanian seeker through one of his churches in Romania.  The next was a man who wrote,   “Hello, my friend sent me this video youtube.com/shorts/4n0LFx63A… As a favor to him I am emailing you. Not fully sure what the challenge is all about. Look forward to hearing from you.”  After the webinar he wrote,   “I prayed along with you. and I will think about this for a while and chew on it. I don’t have any questions yet but will when with mentor.”  Chuck Lloyd will assign the mentor.  The last is a man in Nigeria who wrote, before the webinar:  “Why should I believe in the resurrection of Jesus?”  Post webinar, he indicated that he prayed along with me, and he wrote:  “the mentorship and training would go a long way sir.”  Rice Broocks will assign the mentor for this Nigerian seeker through one of his churches in Nigeria.  Like always, I taught them how to read slowly, prayerfully, and pensively, staring in the Gospel of John. Every morning for 15 minutes. Every morning, not just 3 days per week. We also followed that up with these same instructions in writing sent to each man. Mentors are assigned to all to get them started in one-on-one Bible studies.  Understandably, the acceptance rate for the webinar system is not yet as high as that for one-on-one meetings. So we need to refine the webinar format and strike a balance among several factors for the group sessions: privacy, the ability for them to speak rather than just type questions, the need to protect the identities of those logging in from persecuting countries, and my ability to reach the many that seek to hear from me about the message of Jesus without my getting wiped out (I have a few other day-jobs). So, as Brandon learns the capabilities of the commercial webinar system, may the Lord provide wisdom so we know how best to move forward. Please pray that the Lord provides wisdom and an anointing on those webinar times so that many will be saved.   Lord, I pray, give us the creativity of Bezalel in using the technology, and the passion of Paul for the lost.  While the gospel message will never change! Sinners are saved through the death of the Son of God and his resurrection from the dead. Glorious Jesus!  Romans 10:1 “Brethren, my heart’s desire and my prayer to God for them is for their salvation.”  God bless, Dr. James Tour

„Beast Machine” infrastructure: control is achieved through  “no man might buy or sell”   WARNING   The practical risk case:  crypto/wallets vs. bank deposits No insurance backstop.  Bank deposits are covered by government deposit insurance (FDIC in the US) up to a set limit, but crypto assets — even when held “through” an FDIC-insured bank — get zero protection if the exchange or platform fails (FDIC Advisory via Foodman CPAs, Money).   If your bank fails, you get your money back. If your exchange or wallet provider fails, you may get nothing. Irreversibility is absolute. A fraudulent bank charge can usually be disputed and reversed. A blockchain transaction cannot — send funds to the wrong address, fall for a phishing link, or get hacked, and there is no “reset password” or recovery line (Finance Monthly, Motley Fool).  The custody trap cuts both ways. If you self-custody (hold your own private keys), you own your assets outright — but lose the seed phrase or private key and the funds are gone forever, with no support desk able to help (~20% of all Bitcoin is estimated permanently lost this way) (Omar Moonis — Self-Custody Risk, [Chainalysis via Omar Moonis]). If you use a custodial exchange instead, you’re exposed to that platform’s solvency and security — Mt. Gox, FTX, and the 2025 Bybit hack ($1.46 billion, the largest crypto theft on record) show how catastrophically that can fail (TRM Labs 2026 Crypto Crime Report).  Attack surface is large and growing. In 2025 alone, roughly $2.87 billion was stolen across ~150 hacks; the first months of 2026 already exceed $1 billion lost to state-sponsored hackers (North Korea’s Lazarus Group in particular), AI-driven scams, and smart-contract exploits (TRM Labs, Ledger 2026, 24/7 Wall Street).  A future-facing threat banks don’t face the same way. Quantum computing is considered an “existential threat” to crypto’s cryptography specifically — researchers estimate 35–50% of circulating Bitcoin could be exposed to a quantum attack on public keys, since balances and keys are permanently visible on-chain. Bank ledgers aren’t exposed this way because they aren’t public.[reuters]  No regulator is standing behind you. Banking is a heavily regulated system designed explicitly to reduce insolvency risk and protect depositors; crypto has comparatively thin regulatory cover, meaning “you are on your own” if something goes wrong (Investor.gov Bulletin, Foodman CPAs).  In short: a bank deposit trades some freedom/privacy for institutional insurance, reversibility, and regulatory backstops. A crypto wallet trades those protections for autonomy — and that autonomy comes with unforgiving, irreversible consequences for any single mistake, hack, or platform failure  <<<<>>>>  My dots - Ones you take your cash out of your bank account nobody bother how you spend it.  Your privacy has total anonymity.  Digital cash will be noted exactly - every single movement - you will be recorded in your data sheet - every your breath you will take .   You will think that AI bot is anonymous but it is bs it is the most detailed surveillance possible   And now do your research on running your bank account on platform - it will anyway run through banks   But it is the magic of bots - and how you will be „managed“ or „mismanaged“   You know you are in Matrix.

Fedorov just dropped the receipts before stepping down and it is worth dissecting without the usual victory lap fluff. From 2019 onward he built the bridge between civilian tech momentum and actual battlefield impact. No poetry about unbreakable spirit, just numbers, names, and the structural headaches that still choke results. He called out Trukha directly for the attacks, noted the new Russian-citizen owner of her outlet hunting ways back into drone supply chains for the Ukrainian military, and laid out how digital registries exposed entire brigades where up to half the personnel either went AWOL or filed transfer requests. The servicemen registry is already 95 percent populated. That is not abstract reform, that is forensic accountability finally catching up to Soviet-era personnel black holes.  UNITED24 funded the Army of Drones push, bought serious batches of sea drones that started hitting the Russian fleet, stood up the first strike drone companies and then the Unmanned Systems Forces. The procurement model is blunt: 80 percent of drones bought from the ten most effective producers per category, 20 percent through open tenders that new players can actually enter. One early shell tender cut the 155mm unit price by a thousand dollars and saved a hundred million overall. The current 150,000-drone tender has 59 companies competing with expected 30 percent price compression. Private firms can now test interceptors inside live air defense loops and get paid per confirmed Shahed kill. Some units are already knocking down 90 percent. Add eBals, Brave1 Market as the Amazon for war, performance-based purchases where confirmed strikes earn units more drones, and a billion hryvnias disbursed to the sharpest outfits: Magyar, Alpha, Lazarus group, Achilles, K2. Ground robotic complexes and explosives markets opened too. Twelve thousand NRCs bought in 2025, fifty thousand planned for 2026. Starlink whitelists negotiated with Musk proved a war-turning factor that denied Russians easy drone command links. No full replacement found. Small air defense layers now targeting 95 percent drone kills plus some cruise missiles.  The doctrinal shift Fedorov described is the part Western analysts still half-digest. Technology first destroys enemy positions, then infantry moves. Lose metal, not people. Drone-assault units like Code 9.2 and Khartia retook Kupiansk on that model. Operation Ashan saw 700 Russian armor pieces destroyed at depths up to 50 kilometers; from May to October 2025 Moscow could not mount major mechanized assaults. Ashan-2 took out 250 artillery systems in two nights. The overarching frame is Air-Land-Economy: intercept incoming missiles and drones, halt ground advances, degrade the financial machinery that sustains the war. Territorial losses shrank in 2026 while liberated ground grew. Russian assault tactics degraded from mass mechanized pushes and large infantry groups to small teams or lone operators. That is not narrative, that is measured degradation visible on the sensor logs.  The problems he admitted matter more than the wins for anyone serious about outcomes. Drones, shells, and resources still get handed out manually; loyal commanders eat first. Fedorov openly disagreed with Syrskyi, said Zelenskyy had no plan to replace the commander-in-chief, and that he learned to coexist. After that the General Staff started blocking every proposed reform, personnel move, and project. Fedorov credited Syrskyi for 2022 but stressed the war has mutated while the command culture stayed frozen in authoritarian rigidity. Ukrainian soldiers can and will take responsibility when given resources, clear objectives, and competent leadership. TCK structures report to Ground Forces which report to the commander-in-chief and General Staff. Patching one link solves nothing; the whole chain needs redesign.  Bottom line from the outgoing minister: Ukraine has engineered the technological toolkit that can stop Moscow while shedding fewer lives.

sample_tweets.text: Current Large-Scale Covert Ops in America TODAY ➡️Usual suspects: Russia, China, and Iran  They’re the ones running large-scale covert ops to divide us. The rest mostly just lobby.  Wake up to the real players!  1. Russia (Often the Most Active) ✴️Focus: Sowing domestic discord, undermining elections/democracy, amplifying polarization (racial, political, cultural), and weakening US support for allies like Ukraine. Uses troll farms, state media proxies, Doppelgänger-style fake news sites, hacked accounts, and AI deepfakes. ✴️Scale: High-volume, multilingual operations across platforms (X, Facebook, Telegram, etc.). Persistent even post-2024 election, targeting midterms and ongoing issues. ✴️Why top-tier: Sophisticated at blending covert ops with overt propaganda; frequently cited as the preeminent influence threat in recent cycles.  2. China ✴️Focus: Promoting pro-China narratives, portraying the US as chaotic/racist/divided, countering criticism on Taiwan/Hong Kong/Xinjiang, and targeting down-ballot politicians or policies adversarial to Beijing. Uses networks like Spamouflage, fake journalists/influencers, and state-linked amplification. ✴️Scale: Massive asset footprint (largest in some platform disclosures); leverages diaspora networks, social media, and economic leverage. More cautious on direct election swaying but aggressive on long-term perception management. ✴️Why top-tier: Most capable overall adversary with vast resources; growing use of AI and proxies.  3. Iran ✴️Focus: Exploiting US divisions (e.g., on Israel, elections, Middle East policy), spreading antisemitic/anti-US narratives, discouraging voting, and undermining confidence in institutions. Uses bots, fake personas, memes, and psychological ops, often tied to IRGC-linked efforts. ✴️Scale: Smaller than Russia/China in volume but increasingly sophisticated (especially with AI and proxies); opportunistic on flashpoints like protests or conflicts. ✴️Why top-tier: Aggressive in covert social media and transnational repression; coordinates with Russia/China in some cases.  Other Notable Mentions (Lower Scale) ✴️North Korea: More cyber-focused (hacking, ransomware) but participates in some influence via the broader "Axis" cooperation. ✴️Others (e.g., certain actors from Pakistan, India, or Nigeria-based ops): Occasional smaller-scale or commercial influence, but not on the same systematic, state-directed level as the big three.  These operations are persistent and evolving with AI tools. They often overlap in an "Axis" dynamic (Russia-China-Iran-North Korea cooperation). US responses include platform enforcement, sanctions, and intelligence monitoring, though capabilities have faced changes. For the latest details, refer to ODNI Annual Threat Assessments.

sample_tweets.text: 한국 언급이 없다고 염병 떠는 반국세들아.  "미국의 적대국인 러시아, 북한, 중국 '그 외 세력' 들이 미국의 선거 인프라 침투 능력을 보유 했다."  에서 이미 언급 된거다.  수장이 빨갱이여도 한국은 혈맹인데 대놓고 깔 수 있겠냐? 순서라는게 있지?  쉬운 해킹 가능 이유는 김대중이 뿌린 씨앗임.

[다시 크게 외쳐라 윤어게인!!]  진짜 "윤석열이 결국 옳았다!"라고 목이 터져라 일갈하고 시작해야 된다, 이건.  윤석열 대통령이 감옥에서 쓴 옥중 편지 기억나냐? 거기서 뭐라고 했냐. "공산주의, 네오 마르크즘, 완전히 구축된 전위주의 독재 체제, 초국가 경제 권력... 위장된 민주주의, 부정부패 카르텔, 허위 선동과 가짜 뉴스... 글로벌리즘은 거대한 기득권 카르텔을 구축해 국가도, 주권도, 자유도 거기에 매몰되고 이제는 쉽게 빠져나올 수 없는 지경에 이르렀다"라고 울부짖었잖아.  그 '글로벌리즘의 배신'과 '국제 부정선거 카르텔'의 실체가 뭔지, 이번에 트럼프가 전 세계를 향해 아주 핵폭탄급으로 터뜨려버린 거다.  중공(중국 공산당) 짱깨들이 대규모로 개입해서 미국 유권자 정보 무려 2억 2천만 건을 불법 탈취하고 선거를 조작해왔다는 사실을 트럼프 대통령이 오피셜로 까발렸다고 ㅋㅋㅋ 2억 2천만 건이면 미국 투표권자 대가리 수 그냥 전부 다 털어갔다는 소리인데, 이게 조작용 세팅이 아니면 뭐냐?  더 소름 돋는 건, 미국 정보당국 씹새끼들이 이거 진작에 알고 있었으면서 대통령이었던 트럼프한테도 보고 안 하고 비밀로 싹 묻어두며 은폐했다는 거다. 트럼프가 기밀 해제한 보고서 보니까 투표 기계들 전부 인터넷 망 타고 해킹 공격에 허벌창 수준으로 노출돼 있었고, 러시아·중국·이란·북한 같은 적대국들이 선거 인프라를 쥐락펴락할 능력이 차고 넘친다고 박혀있음.  이게 바로 윤석열 대통령이 말한 '글로벌리즘 기득권 카르텔'의 본질이었던 거임. 미국 정보도 저 지랄로 다 털어가고 장난치는 짱깨 실력자들이, 유권자 몇천만 명밖에 안 되는 한국 정보는 안 털고 가만히 놔뒀겠냐?  예전에 민경욱이 주장했던 '팔로우 더 파티(Follow the party, 중국 공산당 구호)' 지문 발견됐을 때 다들 정병 취급하고 묻어버리려 했고, 국정원 보안점검 때도 한국 선거망 비번이 12345 수준이라 외부 해킹으로 결과값 맘대로 바꿀 수 있다고 경고했잖아. 심지어 이번 지방선거 때 송파에서 투표용지 모자라다고 개난리 친 것도 사전투표 뒤에서 장난질 치다가 뻥 터진 거라는 합리적 의심이 들 수밖에 없다.  자, 묻자.  이렇게 중국 공산당 세력과 글로벌리즘 카르텔이 뒤에서 대한민국 주권과 자유를 야금야금 갉아먹고 선거를 조작하고 있다는 이 엄청난 망국적 사실을 국민에게 알리려고 한 게 도대체 왜 내란이냐? 이 진실을 알리다 감방 가는 게 내란죄냐고 ㅋㅋㅋ  트럼프가 직접 팩트 들이밀고 뚝배기 깨버리니까 "윤석열이 백번 천번 옳았다", "틀딱들 음모론인 줄 알았더니 진짜였다"고 인터넷 뒤집어지는 중이다.  윤석열 대통령이 나라 살리겠다고 목숨 걸고 경고했던 그 거대한 카르텔의 실체가 이제야 만천하에 드러날 시기가 왔다. 레거시 언론 개새끼들 그동안 입 맞춰서 음모론이라고 선동하고 조롱하더니, 미국에서 오피셜 핵폭탄 터진 마당에 이제 이 진실 앞에서 어떻게 대가리 굴릴지 진짜 팝콘각 제대로다 ㅋㅋㅋ

방금 전 끝난 트럼프 대통령의 대국민 연설. 진짜 장난 아니다. 그래서, 한국 언급❓ 직접적인 '한국' 단어 언급은 없었네요. 아무래도 미국 대통령의 대국민 연설인 만큼 미국 선거 안보, 중국·이란 글로벌 패권, 미국의 국익에 초점이 맞춰졌기 때문. 근데 이름만 안 불렀지 누가 봐도 한국을 향한 경고, 간접 저격은 있다고 봐야죠.  기밀 해제된 자료를 언급하며 "중국이 2020년 대선 때부터 2억 2천만 명의 미국 유권자 데이터에 접근해 역사상 가장 큰 선거 데이터 해킹 저질렀다"고 폭로  "선거 보안이 무너지면 국가도 없다"면서 시민권자만 투표할 수 있게 강제하는 'SAVE America Act(미국 구하기 선거법)'를 당장 통과시키라고 의회 압박 중이고  미국 대통령이 전 세계 공산세력의 목을 조르는 거대한 판을 짜놓은 건 틀림없는 것 같습니다.  미국의 도움만을 바라는건 아니었으니까 트럼프의 대국민 연설에 박수를 보내며.. 진짜 위기이니 만큼 청원 동참하고 더 행동으로 보여줘야겠습니다. 대한민국은 우리가 지키는 거니까. 🇺🇸🇰🇷💪
트럼프 대통령 입에서  딥스테이트 말 나온게 진짜 크다.. 이제 부정선거는 공식화 되었다! 중국,북한 등 선거 인프라 해킹 및 조작, 미국의 선거 개입을 본격적으로 조지겠다는 선언.. 선거 시스템 계속 말 나오는 거 보니  곧 한국 차례 오겠다.  너무 기대된다!!😂😭

어쩌면 오늘 트럼프 대통령의 발표는 국가비상사태 선포로 이어질 수 있는 중대한 순간이 될지도 모른다. 이 경우 한국의 부정선거로 인한 비상계엄에 이어 미국발 비상사태가 발령되는 것이다.  그는 새로 기밀 해제된 정보를 공개하겠다고 예고했는데, 지난 6.25 날에 JFK의 부정선거 관련 연설을 게시했었다. "현대전은 침략이 아닌 침투, 선거가 아닌 전복.." 그리고 한국 시간으로 7월 17일 제헌절에 다시 한번 선거 부정에 관한 중대 발표를 앞두고 있다. 이러한 우연은 한국을 염두에 둔 하이브리드 침투에 대한 전쟁 선포로 이해되는 부분이다.  지금까지 트럼프 대통령은 한국 상황을 제2의 6.25 전시 상태로 인식하고 다루어 왔으며 한국의 새로운 건국을 위해 접근해 왔다는 해석이 가능하다.  -2020년 대선에 대한 중국의 간섭  -유권자 등록 데이터베이스 해킹   -광범위한 정보기관의 은폐 작전   -비시민권자가 유권자 명부에 등재된 사실  만약 국가비상사태가 선포된다면, 이는 외국의 부정선거 개입을 단순한 선거 문제로 보지 않고 전쟁 수준의 국가적 위협으로 대응하겠다는 강력한 메시지가 될 것이다.

유럽 금융 사이버 규제, 처벌보다 ‘예방·복구’…EU DORA 시행  이 뉴스가 의미하는 것 해외 금융당국은 해킹 사고 때 벌금 중심보다 예방과 빠른 원상 복구를 더 본대. EU가 지난해 1월부터 시행한 디지털 운영 복원력법(DORA)이 대표 사례야. 한국 금융사 ‘3중 제재’ 논란과 대비해, 유럽은 복원력(resilience) 검증에 초점을 둔다는 비교 기사야.  숨겨진 포인트 - DORA: 대규모 사고 후 금융사가 신속히 복구할 수 있는지를 핵심 기준으로 봄 - EU 27개국 적용, 금융사뿐 아니라 계약 IT 외주업체에도 적용 - 모의 해킹 등으로 보안·운영 체계를 점검하는 방향 - 영국도 ‘운영 복원력’ 규제 체계로 사고 대응·시스템 복원 검증을 강조 - 책임은 분명히 하되 장기 회복·사전 준비에 무게를 둔다는 점이 특징  실제 영향 / 시사점 글로벌 영업 금융사·IT 벤더는 한국형 중복 제재와 유럽형 복원력 규제를 동시에 맞춰야 하는 부담이 커져. 국내에서도 제재 일원화 논의가 나오면, 사후 과징금보다 사전 투자·복구 훈련을 유도하는 쪽이 대안 모델로 거론될 수 있어. 규제 디테일은 프리미엄 본문이 더 있을 수 있어 후속 확인이 필요해.  투자자·트레이더 관점 은행·카드·IT 서비스 쪽 컴플라이언스 비용·외주 리스크 관리 이슈로 읽을 수 있어. 단기 주가 재료보다 중장기 규제 프리미엄·보안 투자 테마 점검용이야. 국내 제재 체계 개편 논의와 쌍으로 보면 금융주 규제 리스크 해석이 더 선명해져.  한 줄 결론 유럽은 금융 해킹 대응에서 처벌보다 예방·복구(DORA 등)에 무게를 두는 규제 철학을 보여 준, 한국 중복 제재 논란 대비 기사야.  출처 : 한국경제  hankyung.com/article/2026071… #DORA #금융보안 #사이버 #EU #복원력  $105560 $055550

👉🇰🇷🇺🇸  강경화 주미대사가 조현 외교부 장관의 지시로 급히 귀국하여 5일간 업무 협의를 가집니다.   주미대사는 보통 휴가 중에도 미국을 떠나지 않는 것이 관례이기에, 지난 4월에 이어 3개월 만에 다시 한국을 찾은 것은 매우 이례적이며 심각한 한미 관계 현안이 발생했음을 시사합니다.  ​현재 한미 간에는 미국 기업인 쿠팡에 대한 차별적 규제 문제로 백악관과 하원 법사위의 압박이 이어지고 있으며, 반도체 투자 이행(조인트 팩트 시트) 갈등 및 안보 현안도 존재합니다.   그러나 이러한 사안들은 전화나 화상 회의로도 협의가 가능합니다. ​따라서 해킹 우려로 통신 보안이 필요한 더 중대한 사안이 있을 것으로 추정됩니다.   특히 한국 시간 17일 오전으로 예정된 트럼프 대통령의 ‘해외의 미국 선거 개입 관련 대국민 연설’에 한국이 언급될 가능성에 대비한 보안 작전 회의이거나, 이재명이의 최근 몽골 방문 등 북한 관련 사안을 포함해 언론에 공개되지 않은 엄중한 한미 갈등이 원인일 가능성이 제기됩니다.

<심층 분석 시리즈> - Long Ver.  *이란 전쟁, 트럼프는 대책 없는 전쟁광이라는 조작 선동에 대하여  4  4-3. 이란은 중국이 미국의 시선을 아시아-태평양에서 분산시키기 위해 사용하는 '카운터 피벗(Counter-Pivot)' 카드이다.  앞서 제시한 보고서를 통해서, AFPI는 에픽 퓨리 작전이 중국과의 패권전쟁에서 큰 승리를 거둔 것으로 평가했다.  이란의 기대와는 달리,  중국은 이란에게 매우 제한적이고 기회주의적인 지원을 하는 데에 그친 것이었다.  이란이 중국의 일대일로에 지정학적 요충지였고, 호르무즈 해협이 중국 석유 수입의 절반에 달하여서, 친중 국가 이란이 호르무즈 해협을 통제하는 것은 예견된 수순이었다는 이야기도 있었다.  그러나, 미국이 실제로 이란에 대규모 공습을 벌이자, 중국은 이란을 그저 할인된 원유를 확보하고, 미국의 시선을 인도 태평양에서 떨어뜨려놓는 ‘카운터 피벗’ 정도로 여겼다는 것이 드러난 것이다.  심지어는 중국이 이란에게 제공해온 위성 네비게이션과 디지털 감시 인프라는, 이스라엘에게 해킹 당하며, 아야톨라 하메네이 등 이란 지도부들을 역추적하는 도구로 사용되었다.  이란 시설 파괴로 인해, 중국이 이란으로부터 할인된 원유를 들여오는 그림자 에너지 무역 또한 붕괴되었다.  그러나, 아직 이란은 항복을 하지 않고 있어, 미국의 대규모 공습이 재개되고 있는 상황이다. 여기서 AFPI는 장기적인 전략으로 다음 세 가지를 제시하였다.  1) 에너지 동맹 재편 대만, 일본, 한국 등 인도-태평양 동맹국들이 중동에 대한 에너지 의존도를 낮추고,  미국산 LNG를 도입하도록 가속화해야 한다.  2) 인지전 및 심리전 강화 에픽 퓨리에서 보여준 미군의 정밀 타격 능력과 중국산 방어 체계의 취약성을 강조하여,  중국 인민해방군이 대만 침공을 엄두 내지 못하도록 억지력을 높여야 한다.  3) 핵심 광물 공급망 자립 무기 생산에 필수적인 핵심 광물의 중국 의존도를 낮추고,  미국 본토 및 우방국(호주, 캐나다, 일본 등) 중심의 안정적인 자체 공급망을 시급히 확보해야 한다.

sample_tweets.text: Hilly Clinton, the former Secretary of State that has spoken with Chinese leadership one on one, boldly encourages them to cyber attack us.  And then---get this, it's nuts... We got cyber attacked.  Cast her into Tartarus until the last star in the sky burns out.

Mac Users Beware! New Malware Is Stealing Crypto Wallets!! A blockchain security company called SlowMist has warned crypto investors about a dangerous new malware targeting macOS users. The malware, known as MacSync Stealer, secretly infects Mac computers and steals valuable information such as crypto wallet files, browser passwords, saved login credentials, system keychains, and even cloud access keys. Once hackers gain access to this information, they can drain cryptocurrency wallets or take control of important online accounts without the victim realizing it until it's too late.  This attack is a reminder that Mac computers are no longer immune to cyber threats. Crypto investors are especially attractive targets because stolen wallets can result in irreversible financial losses. Security experts recommend downloading software only from trusted sources, enabling two-factor authentication, keeping macOS updated, and never storing recovery phrases digitally. Even experienced crypto users can become victims if they install fake applications or click malicious links. As digital assets become more valuable, cybercriminals are constantly developing smarter ways to steal them, making good cybersecurity habits more important than ever.

As we look toward the widespread adoption of robots in the future, how does the technology stack of Tesla’s Optimus compare to that of robots from other manufacturers—such as Figure AI—in terms of defending against cyberattacks? At present, Tesla’s Optimus appears to hold the lead.  Tesla’s full-stack self-developed technology (chip + inference engine + model) provides a notable differentiation in cybersecurity for robots, especially as humanoids become widespread. Here’s a clear comparison with companies like Figure AI and others. 1. Tesla Optimus: Integrated, Closed, Full-Stack Approach Key Security Strengths: •Smaller Attack Surface: Everything from the custom AI chips (AI4/AI5), inference engine, to the end-to-end neural network models is developed in-house. This reduces reliance on third-party components (e.g., ROS 2 middleware, standard Linux distributions, or off-the-shelf TensorRT), minimizing common vulnerabilities at interfaces. •Hardware-Level Redundancy: The AI4 chip uses dual parallel systems that continuously check each other. If one fails or is compromised, the other can take over instantly — a design inherited from automotive FSD safety standards (ASIL, UN-R155 cybersecurity regulations). •Secure Boot & Firmware Control: Strong hardware Root of Trust, signed firmware updates, and Tesla’s ability to remotely disable compromised features or push fixes fleet-wide (they’ve already done this for unauthorized FSD modifications). •Data-Driven Robustness: Massive real-world data (from vehicles + factories) trains the models against adversarial attacks and edge cases. Safety is baked into the learned behavior rather than added as rules. Potential Weaknesses: •Black-Box Risk: Less external auditing possible. A deep zero-day in the core engine could have broad impact. •Insider/Supply Chain: Heavy reliance on internal security; 2. Figure AI and Most Other Vendors: Modular, Open Ecosystem Typical Setup: •Heavy use of NVIDIA Jetson hardware + TensorRT. •Often built on ROS 2 or similar middleware, with more third-party libraries and cloud services. •Transitioning to neural controllers (Figure’s Helix is moving toward more end-to-end), but still more layered than Tesla. Security Characteristics: •Larger Attack Surface: More components = more potential entry points (ROS topic spoofing, replay attacks, Bluetooth/Wi-Fi provisioning flaws seen in competitors like Unitree). •Easier External Auditing: More open stack means security researchers can find and report issues faster (good for patching). •Reliance on Supply Chain: Security depends on NVIDIA’s updates, open-source middleware hardening (SROS2), etc. Some vendors have had serious flaws (hardcoded keys, unauthorized telemetry, wormable exploits turning robots into physical botnets). Current Efforts: •Figure is actively hiring security engineers focused on embedded/robot security, vulnerability management, and cloud/robot backend protection — showing awareness of the gaps. 3. Key Differences in a Mass-Adoption Future •Tesla Advantage: ◦Faster Fleet Response: Over-the-air control + unified stack allows quick isolation or patching across thousands/millions of units. ◦Physical-Cyber Convergence: Better integration of safety (preventing harmful actions) and security (preventing hacks that cause harm). ◦Regulatory Edge: Easier compliance with strict standards •Other Companies’ Challenges: ◦Higher risk of “physical botnets” or cascading failures if one popular middleware/component is compromised. ◦More fragmentation in security updates Tesla’s full-stack approach gives it a structural edge in cybersecurity for high-stakes physical interactions (factories, homes), where a breach isn’t just data loss — it can cause real-world injury. Other firms can catch up with strong zero-trust, TPM/FPGA root-of-trust, and AI-driven anomaly detection, but they start with a more complex, heterogeneous attack surface.

Iran just admitted its power grid is under attack for the first time ever. They're asking citizens to cut electricity use by 25%. This is not a drill - and the energy market has barely noticed $SOXL yet.  Here's what just happened: Iran's government acknowledged coordinated attacks on critical power infrastructure. Not cyber espionage. Not sanctions pressure. Active attacks. The same week their grid was already straining from summer demand. Now they're rationing electricity to prevent total blackout.  The market implications most people miss: When a major energy producer loses domestic power generation capacity, two things spike - oil prices as backup generation fails, and semiconductor/tech stocks as the US energy independence narrative gets stronger. $SOXL should be pricing in the geopolitical tail risk premium that comes with destabilized Middle Eastern energy. It's not.  We've seen this play before. 2019 Saudi Aramco attacks sent crude up 19% overnight. But those were attacks on production. This is different - this is a government admitting its power infrastructure is compromised. That signals a deeper vulnerability.  The real question: Are you positioning for oil volatility and the energy security premium that typically lifts semiconductor stocks when Middle East stability cracks? Or waiting to see if markets catch up to what Tehran just confessed?  #Energy #Geopolitics

SECURITY ALERT: SlowMist has warned of a new macOS malware capable of hijacking Telegram sessions and targeting crypto wallets.  The malware is designed to steal sensitive data, putting crypto users at risk of account compromise and fund theft.   Mac users are urged to stay cautious, avoid downloading untrusted software, and enable additional security measures.

A 21-year-old man from Florida has been arrested and charged in connection with a malware campaign that allegedly used Steam games to steal cryptocurrency from players.  According to federal prosecutors, the suspect is accused of helping distribute 8 malware-infected games on Steam between May 2024 and January 2026.   The FBI says the campaign targeted around 8,000 users and stole at least $220,000 worth of cryptocurrency from roughly 80 crypto wallets.  The games identified by the FBI include BlockBlasters, Chemia, Dashverse/DashFPS, Lampy, Lunara, PirateFi, and Tokenova.  All have since been removed from Steam and are flagged as suspicious in SteamDB archives. PirateFi was previously removed by Valve after reports that it contained malware.

Spy and Villain by Joy Lilley, is a compelling short story that has touched readers with its poignan

sample_tweets.text: In total vulnerability lies unbreakable power.

Spy and Villain by Joy Lilley, is a compelling short story that has touched readers with its poignant blend of suspense, compassion, and quiet heroism, earning praise for its heartfelt portrayal of observation, empathy, and the triumph of kindness over suspicion in a tale infused with gentle Christian themes. This concise yet impactful narrative centers on Mavis, a physically handicapped woman confined to a wheelchair following an unfortunate accident, who spends her days discreetly observing the world from behind her curtains. When she becomes convinced that her neighbor across the road is enduring abuse—marked by troubling signs and the woman's eventual disappearance—Mavis refuses to dismiss her instincts, even as her husband brushes off her concerns. Determined to uncover the truth, Mavis transforms into an unlikely "spy," venturing beyond her home to befriend the vulnerable woman, leading to a dramatic revelation that proves her right and forges a meaningful, supportive friendship amid ongoing distress.  Readers have appreciated the story's emotional warmth and moral depth despite its brevity. An Amazon reviewer shared, “A charming short story... the lady in the wheelchair who spends much of her time looking at others through her window,” highlighting its engaging premise. Another noted the Christian undertones that guide the protagonist's actions toward redemption and connection, with one calling it “a tale of a spy... proved to be right. And becomes a friend of the abused woman.” Promotional features on blogs like Book Marketing Global Network and Something or Other Publishing emphasize its dramatic turn and themes of suspicion turning to solidarity, while its availability on Kindle Unlimited has helped it reach fans of inspirational short fiction. With a solid 3.6-star average on Amazon from early readers, it stands out for its relatable character and uplifting resolution.  @joygerken zurl.co/6meHt zurl.co/BrJIs zurl.co/qPjsN zurl.co/PfzcK zurl.co/SJwFK zurl.co/1gRn9    Joy M. Lilley, a British author and retired registered nurse (real name Joy Gerken), has built a devoted following with her empathetic, character-focused stories that draw from life's challenges and triumphs. Residing in Kent—the garden of England—and a proud grandmother, her nursing background at places like the Royal Victoria Hospital lends authentic insight into human vulnerability, which shines in her diverse works spanning romance, historical fiction, contemporary drama, and short tales. Acclaimed titles include *Figs, Vines and Roses*, *The Liberty Bodice*, *Time's Pendulum Swings Again*, *Strawberry Moon*, *Joyful Pussy Cat Tales*, and *Bleeds When Broken*. Reviewers commend her graceful handling of sensitive topics, with one praising her ability to weave “compelling narratives around complex issues.” Inspired by family, faith, and her peaceful surroundings, Lilley connects warmly with readers through her blog and social media, offering stories that inspire reflection and hope.  Spy and Villain is a touching inspirational short story, its themes of vigilance, friendship, and faith-driven compassion affirming its appeal for readers seeking quick, meaningful reads with heart. Joy M. Lilley’s skillful blend of suspense and tenderness makes this compact gem a shining example of her compassionate storytelling legacy.

joe locke as charlie spring: “heartstopper forever” review round-up  “…joe locke imbues charlie with a quiet resi­li­ence. together, they cre­ate a bond so con­vin­cing that it sur­vives even the script and score’s occa­sional habit of over­ex­plain­ing what their faces already reveal.”  🔗: nytimes.com/2026/07/17/movie…  “as charlie, joe locke is endearing wearing his emotions on his sleeve as a sensitive gay teen.”  🔗: epgn.com/2026/07/17/heartsto…  “…joe locke portrays charlie with a confidence that never ignores the character's lingering vulnerabilities. their chemistry remains the emotional heart of the film, but it is now built on difficult conversations, silence and compromise rather than the excitement of first love.”  🔗: en.xmag.live/heartstopper-fo…  “locke and connor are tuned into their respective characters by now, but bring out more dimensions to them as charlie and nick are forced to grow up. […] the actors’ seamless chemistry anchors heartstopper‘s fittingly lovely goodbye.”  🔗: avclub.com/heartstopper-fore…  “joe locke and kit connor have quite rightly been two of the biggest breakout stars of this decade thanks to heartstopper. so as the curtain closes on nick and charlie, watching these two young stars channelling everything they’ve absorbed over the last four years – from working on huge box office blockbusters, marvel series and broadway shows and elevating as actors who were once finding their feet to being so much more powerful in their performances than they were just four years ago – is really quite moving.”  🔗: metro.co.uk/2026/07/17/heart…  “it is clear that joe locke and kit connor have never felt more comfortable as nick and charlie, making their chemistry feel stronger than ever. with a relationship built on care, understanding and support for each other and those around them, it is difficult not to root for the pair and their heartfelt coming-of-age journey.”  🔗: poppednews.com/2026/07/17/he…  “locke and connor remain the standout additions. their chemistry is so strong that even those who’ve never seen an episode will be able to understand how much they’ve been through. each actor gets plenty of time to shine, with their performances carrying the emotional weight of the script flawlessly. although the writing can verge ever so slightly into teenage melodrama, the stars ensure that their reactions are fully grounded and relatable, which leads to moments that are both euphoric and heartbreaking. […] locke and connor are spectacular in the final farewell to the characters that stole the hearts of fans.”  🔗: theupcoming.co.uk/2026/07/17…  “yet, heartstopper forever, much like the show that came before it, succeeds because of the chemistry between locke and connor. charlie and nick’s relationship is just as gripping as it was in the series, and in the film, the stars are better than ever in these roles. joe locke, especially, delivers what may be the strongest performance of his career thus far, with a turn that feels both sincere and vulnerable.”  🔗: screenrant.com/heartstopper-…  “up until now, the show has rarely interrogated the stability of their romance, but these confrontations are also a showcase of how locke and connor have matured as performers.”  🔗: radiotimes.com/tv/drama/hear…  “this is all about charlie and nick, and the young actors in those roles play this duo perfectly. the importance of the portrayal is that joe locke and kit connor need to capture youth and love in all of the ways that it appears. anger, jealousy, hope, wonder, and of course ample amounts of vulnerability. while this is captured through their dialogue, it’s their moments of intimacy that carry far more emotions than just what we hear them say.”  🔗: butwhytho.net/2026/07/hearts…  “the returning leads carry a history that no amount of writing could fake, and it becomes its own kind of stake. kit connor and joe locke have grown up inside these roles in plain sight, and so has the audience that started watching them at fourteen.”  🔗: martincid.com/movies/heartst…  “kit connor and joe locke continue to have incredible chemistry that never misses a beat. for joe, the thing that’s striking is the eating disorder bits, which couldn’t have been easy to work through.”  🔗: dmtalkies.com/heartstopper-f…  “kit connor and joe locke have both grown tremendously as actors, and it’s beautiful to watch them inhabit nick and charlie once again. they’re our nick and charlie forever. both deliver layered, deeply moving, and heartfelt performances that make this final chapter all the more emotional.”  🔗: thefutureoftheforce.com/2026…  “thanks to locke and connor’s beautiful performances, watching the characters navigate these challenges is both heartbreaking and heartwarming. one key example is a conservatory scene that sees nick and charlie struggle to communicate — a trait that has made their relationship so great. in contrast, an almost-dialogue-free sequence between them at the beach delivers an irresistible fuzziness. even amid the hard times, it is hard to deny the charm of nick and charlie’s romance.”  🔗: flickfeast.co.uk/reviews/fil…  “...with locke ably navigating this worrisome subplot […] as led by beautiful turns from connor and locke...”  🔗: screenhub.com.au/news/review…  “kit connor and joe locke deliver some of the strongest work of the entire series. their chemistry has always felt less like television romance than genuine familiarity—the kind that only comes from years of growing alongside one another both on screen and off. heartstopper forever trusts that chemistry completely. rather than leaning on grand speeches or melodramatic confrontations, it allows hesitant silences, lingering glances, awkward laughter, and everyday routines to carry the emotional weight. the result is a relationship that never feels performed so much as lived.”  🔗: qplusmagazine.com/heartstopp…

⚠️  7-Zip Vulnerability Exposes Millions of Users to Remote Code Execution Risk   Source: cybersecuritynews.com/7-zip-…  A newly disclosed vulnerability in 7-Zip, one of the most widely used open-source file archiving tools, could allow remote attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected systems.  Tracked as CVE-2026-14266, the flaw stems from improper handling of XZ chunked data and has been addressed in the latest software update. The vulnerability resides in how 7-Zip processes XZ-compressed data streams.  Specifically, specially crafted XZ chunked data can trigger a heap-based buffer overflow, a memory corruption issue that occurs when data written to a buffer exceeds its allocated space.  #cybersecuritynews #vulnerability

Bleeds When Broken by Joy M. Lilley, released in August 2024, is a poignant contemporary drama that has deeply moved readers with its raw exploration of trauma, self-harm, mental health struggles, and the arduous path to healing and self-discovery, earning high praise for its unflinching honesty and emotional resonance. Centered on protagonist Fiona, a young woman scarred by childhood abuse and ongoing battles with depression and self-destructive behaviors, the novel follows her heart-wrenching journey through pain, dysfunctional relationships, therapy, and tentative steps toward redemption. As Fiona confronts buried secrets, toxic influences, and the lingering wounds that "bleed when broken," Lilley's character-driven narrative fearlessly tackles taboo subjects, blending despair with glimmers of hope in a story that underscores the resilience of the human spirit amid profound vulnerability. Readers have commended the novel's powerful authenticity and compassionate insight. A Goodreads reviewer noted its exceptional impact, contributing to an impressive average rating of nearly 5 stars from early readers. On Amazon, a verified purchaser described it as “A dark tale,” awarding 4 stars and appreciating the depth of its emotional portrayal. Promotional highlights emphasize its “raw and unvarnished” approach, with one editorial review calling it “a deeply moving portrayal of Fiona... marked by the scars of self-harm,” praising Lilley's skill in weaving complex themes into a compelling, thought-provoking read. X posts and social media shares in late 2025 celebrate its poignant narrative and availability on Kindle Unlimited, amplifying its reach among those seeking stories of survival and recovery @joygerken zurl.co/O7op4 zurl.co/rxwiV zurl.co/cA6z7 zurl.co/T8NAl zurl.co/54nI3   Joy M. Lilley, a British author and retired registered nurse (real name Joy Gerken), has established herself as a sensitive chronicler of human experiences, drawing from her professional background to infuse her stories with profound empathy and realism. Living in Kent—the garden of England—and a cherished grandmother, her nursing career enriched her understanding of emotional and physical wounds, which she channels into diverse genres including romance, historical tales, and contemporary dramas. Acclaimed works like *Figs, Vines and Roses*, *The Liberty Bodice*, *Time's Pendulum Swings Again*, *Strawberry Moon*, and the whimsical *Joyful Pussy Cat Tales* showcase her versatility. Reviewers laud her ability to handle sensitive topics with grace, with one highlighting how she “weaves a compelling narrative around complex issues.” Inspired by life, family, and her serene surroundings, Lilley engages avidly with readers through her blog and social platforms, delivering narratives that heal as much as they reveal. Bleeds When Broken is a courageous and heartfelt drama, its unflinching dive into trauma and triumph affirming its essential place for readers seeking authentic stories of mental health, resilience, and renewal. Joy M. Lilley’s masterful handling of delicate wounds marks this novel as a luminous addition to her compassionate literary legacy.

The $14.5 billion acquisition of Element Solutions by Solstice is more than a corporate transaction.  It is a clear expression of the 2024–2026 friend-shoring wave — consolidating two non-substitutable layers of the AI hardware supply chain under one U.S. entity.  Solstice is combining Element Solutions’ electronic chemicals (critical for semiconductor packaging and PCBs) with its own high-performance, low-GWP liquid coolants for AI data centers.  ⚠️Risk #1 Single Point of Failure:  These specialized chemicals often require climate-controlled ISO tanks with limited alternative suppliers. Any disruption — cyber, physical, or sanctions-related — would create an immediate bullwhip effect on shipping.  🚨Risk #2 Chokepoint Concentration:  The consolidated supply chain increases the systemic importance of: 🔺 Trans-Pacific shipping lanes to Taiwan, South Korea and Japan. 🔺Trans-Atlantic routes to European data centers   🔺West Coast ports in the United States  🆘Risk #3 HazMat & Regulatory Concentration:  Limited number of corporate conduits for shipping increases vulnerability to any regulatory action, export controls or port-state detention. This will increase demand for specialized containers, chartering and insurance.  ⛴️Other maritime risks worth monitoring: 🔺Higher war-risk and political-risk insurance premiums. 🔺Potential weaponization of antitrust reviews (especially in China). 🔺Inventory buffering of semiconductor and data center customers.  Solstice-related chemical transportation is therefore now to be considered systemic to the buildout of AI infrastructure.  @NSCG2025 continues to monitor how consolidation in critical materials is reshaping maritime risk exposure.  More analysis at 🌐 nsconsultancyglobal.com #GeopoliticalRisk #MaritimeSecurity #SupplyChain #FriendShoring #AIinfrastructure #Semiconductors #TechNationalism #CriticalMaterials #NSCG2025

if someone can easily break the chain, crash everything, or steal/freeze big money, that’s a critical L1 bug.  the @PushChain bug bounty programme with @hackenclub just went live with you to $70,000 in rewards   the programme is focused on critical and high impact bugs.  critical vulnerability means it’s both easy to do and causes really bad damage  below is what hunters should look out for :  easy to pull off where; → the attacker doesn’t need any special admin powers to start the attack. → they might be able to steal control of an admin account if the bug lets them → the attacker doesn’t need a ton of tokens or a lot of money. → doesn’t need crazy computers or hours/days to do it.  big damage and impact : → the bug can mess up the whole network badly, like lots of nodes crashing all at once. → overloading nodes so they can’t work anymore (even if they don’t fully crash). → stopping new transactions from going through or getting confirmed. → stealing or losing a lot of money, or locking funds forever so no one can use them. → breaking the consensus system so one person can control most nodes and decide which blocks get accepted or rejected.  the bug is about all-round check on the pushchain L1 to create a trustable  mainnet atmosphere   #PusChainAudit the end is near!  start on the link in the comment section

sample_tweets.text: [단독] 트럼프 공개 문서 “AWEB 아닌 '다크웹'이 진짜 위협”  2020 미국 선거 인프라 취약점 보고서가 트럼프 대통령 승인으로 공개됐다. DNI 개버드가 분류 해제한 문건은 러시아·중국·이란·북한이 투표 시스템 공격 능력을 갖췄다고 경고한다.  중앙 선거 데이터 저장소(유권자 DB·투표 기록 등)가 가장 취약하며 접근 시 투표 방해와 결과 신뢰 붕괴가 가능하다.  애국계정 형상기억드럼통 분석에 따르면 구체적 조작 계획은 확인되지 않았으나, 'A-WEB이 아닌 다크웹'에서 공격 도구를 쉽게 구할 수 있어 비국가 해커까지 위협이 크다고 지적했다.  미국 선거의 구조적 허점을 드러낸 충격 문서로 파장이 이어질 전망이다.  (사진: 개버드 기밀 문서 번역본)

🇺🇸 2020 미국 선거 인프라 취약점  - 작성일: 2020년 1월 15일 (NICM 2020-003) - 분류 해제: 2026년 3월 16일 DNI Gabbard에 의해 - 공개 승인: 2026년 7월 3일 트럼프 대통령에 의해  주요 내용 요약 - 적대 세력: 러시아, 중국, 이란, 북한을 미국 선거 인프라(투표 시스템)를 공격할 능력이 있다고 판단. - 중앙 선거 데이터 저장소(유권자 등록 DB, 투표소 기록, 공식 선거 사이트 등)가 가장 취약. - 이러한 시스템에 접근하면 투표 과정 방해, 민감 데이터 탈취, 선거 결과 신뢰 저하 등이 가능. - 다만, 구체적인 조작 계획이 있는지는 확인되지 않았다고 명시. - A-WEB이 아닌 Dark Web에서 공격 도구를 쉽게 구할 수 있어 비국가 행위자(해커 등)도 위협이 될 수 있음.  ⚠️ A-WEB이 아닌 Dark Web 👾

인공지능(AI)은 사이버 보안 시장에 역사적인 호황.  생성형 AI는 악성코드 제작, 피싱 문구 생성, 취약점 탐색과 공격 자동화에 필요한 기술 진입 장벽을 크게 낮췄다.   전문 해커 조직이 필요했던 공격을 이제는 상대적으로 낮은 비용과 짧은 시간 안에 수행할 수 있게 됨.  공격의 빈도와 정교함이 동시에 높아지면서 기업과 정부의 보안 투자는 선택이 아니라 필수인 상황으로 바뀌고 있다.  전 세계 사이버 보안 시장은 2025년 약 2,276억$에서 2030년 약 3,519억$로 성장할 전망, 연평균 성장률(CAGR)은 9.1%에 달할 것으로 예상.  과거엔 사이버 보안이 경기 상황에 따라 조절 가능한 IT 지출에 가까웠으나 앞으로 AI 인프라와 클라우드 확산을 유지하기 위해 반드시 지출해야 하는 핵심 인프라 비용으로 주목받고 있음.

트럼프 대통령, 2020년 대선 취약점 보고서 전격 공개…“러·중·이·북, 미 선거 인프라 침해 역량 보유했었다  도널드 J. 트럼프 대통령의 승인을 거쳐 전격 공개된 국가정보위원회(NIC)의 기밀 해제 문서가 정치권에 거센 파장을 일으키고 있다. 2020년 1월 15일에 작성된 기밀 메모덤(NICM 2020-03) ‘미국 2020년 선거 인프라의 취약점’에 따르면, 미국 정보당국은 지난 2020년 대선 당시 적대국들이 미국의 선거 시스템을 무력화하거나 침해할 수 있는 충분한 사이버 역량을 갖추고 있었던 것으로 평가했다.  해당 문서는 가바드 국가정보국장(DNI)에 의해 올해 3월 16일 비밀이 해제되었으며, 지난 7월 3일 도널드 J. 트럼프 대통령의 최종 승인을 받아 대중에 전격 공표되었다. 본보는 이번에 공개된 정보당국의 메모덤 내용 중 ‘미국 선거 인프라 및 적대국들의 위협’에 관한 핵심 내용을 집중 분석했다.  4대 적대국, 美 선거 시스템 침해 및 조작 역량 상시 보유 정보당국은 보고서에서 러시아, 중국, 이란, 북한 등 4개 국가와 비국가 단체들이 2020년 미국 대통령 선거 관련 인프라를 침해할 수 있는 기술적 역량을 보유하고 있다고 판단했다. 이들 국가가 선거 관련 컴퓨터 시스템 내부의 데이터를 잠재적으로 조작할 수 있는 능력이 있다는 점을 명시한 것이다. 다만, 문서 작성 시점을 기준으로 이들이 실제로 선거 시스템을 조작하려는 구체적인 실행 계획을 수립했는지 여부는 확인되지 않았다고 덧붙였다.  유권자 등록 DB·공식 웹사이트가 ‘최대 취약점’ 보고서는 선거 인프라 중에서도 특히 ‘중앙 집중식 데이터 저장소’를 가장 취약한 요소로 꼽았다. 구체적으로는 ▲유권자 등록 데이터베이스, ▲전자 선거인명부(pollbooks), ▲정부의 공식 선거 웹사이트 등이 적대 세력의 해킹이나 착취에 노출될 위험이 가장 크다고 진단했다. 만약 해커들이 이 같은 중앙 시스템에 접근할 경우, 선거 프로세스 전반이 마비되거나 극심한 혼란에 직면할 수 있다는 경고다.  반면, 표를 집계하고 전송·표시하는 ‘개표 및 결과 표출 시스템’의 경우 국지적인 수준의 해킹이나 조작은 가능할지 몰라도, 대선 결과 전체를 뒤바꿀 만큼 광범위한 규모로 조작하는 것은 기술적으로 매우 어려울 것으로 내다봤다.  “조작했다”는 주장 자체로도 선거 신뢰도 붕괴 위험 특히 정보당국은 적대 세력이 실제로 시스템 내부 데이터를 조작하지 않았더라도, 심리전 차원에서 “우리가 선거 결과를 조작하는 데 성공했다”고 허위 주장을 펼치는 시나리오를 심각하게 우려했다. 이러한 기만 전술은 사실 여부를 신속하게 반박하기가 극도로 어려우며, 그 자체만으로도 선거 결과에 대한 미국 대중의 신뢰를 심각하게 무너뜨릴 수 있다는 지적이다.  과거 러시아의 선거 개입 전례 재확인 미 정보당국은 적대국들의 사이버 위협이 단순한 가상의 시나리오가 아님을 분명히 했다. 보고서에 따르면 러시아는 지난 2016년 대선 주기 당시 미국의 모든 주(State) 선거 네트워크를 대상으로 정찰(reconnoitered) 활동을 벌였으며, 최소 2개 주 이상의 선거 관련 인프라에 직접 접근했다. 심지어 최소 1개 주에서는 유권자 데이터를 외부로 탈취(exfiltrated)했던 전례가 있음이 본 기밀 문서를 통해 다시 한번 공식 확인되었다.  정보당국은 이러한 과거 전적과 고도화된 사이버 역량을 바탕으로, 러시아를 비롯한 중국, 이란, 북한 등 4개국 모두가 2020년 대선에서도 언제든 유사한 선거 방해 공작을 수행할 수 있다고 결론지었다. 이와 함께 다크웹을 통해 정교한 네트워크 침입 도구가 무분별하게 확산되면서, 일반 사이버 범죄자나 핵티비스트들까지 금전적·정치적 목적을 위해 선거 인프라를 공격 타깃으로 삼을 가능성이 상존한다고 경고했다.   justthenews.com/politics-pol…

sample_tweets.text: 얘들아!!!!!! 트위터 해킹 조심해라!!!!!!  1. 앱/웹 싹 다 로그아웃 시킴 2. 메일 변경함 3. 2FA 걸어둠 (두 번째 사진)  원래는 해킹 당해도 계정 만든 이메일로 문의 보내면 새 비번 만드는 링크 보내줘서 해결 됐었는데 2단계 걸어놔서 그거 쫀득하게 막아둔거임  내 친구 계정 지금 대신 해결해주고 있는데 방법이 트위터 서포트랑 2FA 해제 건으로 이메일로 얘기하는 수 밖에 없음 ㅁㅊ 요즘 유출된 거 하도 많아서 그런가봐 주변에 해킹소식 개많이 들려....... 미리 비번 바꾸고 2차 인증걸어놔 꼭....ㅠㅜㅡㅜ  해결되면 해결 방법 써올게 해킹범 이 미친것...

번역전달자들은 내용을 그대로 직역하여 전달하는 문제의 한계지만..  트럼프의 메시지중 중요한것은  ⬇️ 트럼프: “이 보고서들은 우리 정부가 오랫동안 이 선거기계들이 해킹 공격에 극도로 취약하다는 것을 알고 있었다는 것을 보여준다  보고서에서는 러시아, 중국, 이란, 북한을 포함한 미국의 적대국들과 비국가 단체들이 미국 선거 인프라를 [손상시킬 능력을 가지고 있다]고 명시되어 있다.”  ⬇️  전달자의 전달 내용을 명확히 이해하실 수 있는 분들이라면 트럼프의 발표를 보다 명확히 이해하셨을겁니다  이 의미는 단순히 특정 국가들이 저질렀음만을 시사하는 내용이 아닙니다.  그것은 트럼프에게 전달 된 [보고서]의 내용입니다.  그것은 그 국가들의 가능성을 시사합니다.  그럼 여기서 곧 이어질 내용들은 해당 국가들의 해명, 그리고 그 국가들중에서도 특정 몇몇 국가들이 제공하는 또 다른 정보로 이어집니다  그것은 곧 “공조”로 이어질 것 입니다  트럼프는 중국과 딥스테이트가 이 반국가행위를 저질렀다고 얘기했을뿐  그 외 언급한 국가들이 저질렀다고 얘기하지 않았습니다  그 인프라에 연결되어 있는 [연관성]을 얘기했습니다  트럼프의 메시지는 단순, “누가 저질렀다”가 아니라 “우리 정부가 오래전부터 취약점을 알면서도 숨겼다 + 전통적인 적대국들이 능력을 가지고 있다” 에 초점을 맞춥니다.  트럼프는 보고서의 내용을 전달했습니다.  모든 이야기는 [현재진행형] 입니다.  린지-그레이엄의 사망 소식 역시, 여러분에게 하나의 큰 증거로 적용될 것 입니다.  “결과가 증명할 것 입니다.”  확대해석은 불-필요 합니다.  “결과가 증명할 것 입니다.”  가장 궁극적인 진짜 적은 [딥스테이트] 세력입니다.  그리고, 트럼프가 이번에도 언급했던 [글로벌엘리트] 입니다.  지켜보세요  모두가 이해하시게 될 것 입니다  결국, [결과]가 [증명]할 것 입니다.  Sum of all fears.
