#threatreport #MediumCompleteness CVE-2025-54068 Laravel Livewire Credential Theft Campaign: 6,000+ Applications Compromised | 23-06-2026 Source: imperva.com/blog/cve-2025-54… Key details below ↓  🎯Victims: E commerce, Healthcare, Financial services, Education, Government, Online gambling and betting, Logistics  🏭Industry: Government, Healthcare, E-commerce, Education, Logistic  🌐Geo: Asia, Indonesian, Asian, Brazilian  🔓CVEs: CVE-2025-54068  \[[Vulners](vulners.com/cve/CVE-2025-540…)]  - CVSS V3.1: *9.8*,   - Vulners: Exploitation: True     Soft:  - laravel livewire (<3.6.4)  📚TTPs: ⚔️Tactics: 4 🛠️Technics: 11  🧨IOCs:  - Url: 1  - File: 2  - Hash: 1  - IP: 1  - Domain: 1  - Email: 1  💽Software: Laravel Livewire, Livewire, Laravel, telegram, Unix, curl  🔢Algorithms: zip, sha256  📜Programming Languages: php  #threatreport: On May 24, 2026, a major credential theft campaign exploiting CVE-2025-54068 was observed targeting Laravel Livewire applications, primarily affecting versions up to v3.6.3. This critical vulnerability arises from inadequate validation of component property updates during the hydration process, which allows unauthenticated attackers to inject malicious serialized PHP objects leading to arbitrary code execution upon deserialization. The attacker leveraged this flaw to execute a payload that fetched and executed a Bash script from their command-and-control (C2) server.  The captured payload indicated that the attacker used PHPGGC gadget chains, which exploit existing legitimate PHP classes within Laravel applications. The malicious Bash shell script, identified as shoc.enz, was a lightweight 5,269 bytes in size, and served as a credential stealer. Once executed, it set up a temporary working directory, ensured no other instances were running, searched for sensitive .env files containing crucial configuration data, archived these files, and subsequently exfiltrated them to multiple C2 channels, while also cleaning up to erase forensic traces.  Analysis revealed that over 6,167 applications across diverse sectors, including e-commerce, healthcare, financial services, and even governmental bodies, had their credentials compromised. The extant data included more than 1,850 database dumps and extensive email lists, indicating the active exploitation of stolen credentials. Indicators attributing the campaign to an Indonesian threat actor included linguistic elements in the malware’s code and metadata associated with the C2 infrastructure, including Telegram handles and an email address linked to multiple prior breaches in underground forums.  The targeted applications encompassed a wide array of Laravel deployments, including platforms related to online gambling, education, and logistics, thereby underscoring the indiscriminate nature of the scanning efforts. Any organization utilizing unpatched Laravel Livewire v3 versions was potential prey for this extensive campaign. Overall, the operation highlights significant vulnerabilities within widely used frameworks and the severe implications of their exploitation in the cyber realm.

#threatreport #HighCompleteness GhostShell (MB-0009): Targeting Ukraine’s UAV Operations and Defense Supply Chain | 22-06-2026 Source: blog.synapticsystems.de/ghos… Key details below ↓  🧑‍💻Actors/Campaigns: Uac-0244 Gamaredon  💀Threats: Supply_chain_technique, Ghostshell, Kraken_cryptor, Mantis_botnet, Metasploit_tool, Process_injection_technique, Xray_tool, Native_loader, Vidar_stealer, Dead_drop_technique, Antidebugging_technique, Spear-phishing_technique,  🎯Victims: Ukraine, Uav operations, Drone supply chain, Military units, Technical personnel, Procurement staff, Volunteer organizations, Defense sector partners  🏭Industry: Healthcare, Military  🌐Geo: Ukraine, Kazakhstan, Ukraines, Moldova, Russia, German, Germany, Spain, Ukrainian  🔓CVEs: CVE-2025-8088  \[[Vulners](vulners.com/cve/CVE-2025-808…)]  - CVSS V3.1: *8.8*,   - Vulners: Exploitation: True     Soft:  - rarlab winrar (<7.13)  CVE-2025-6218  \[[Vulners](vulners.com/cve/CVE-2025-621…)]  - CVSS V3.1: *7.8*,   - Vulners: Exploitation: True     Soft:  - rarlab winrar (<7.12)  📚TTPs: ⚔️Tactics: 7 🛠️Technics: 14  🧨IOCs:  - File: 15  - Domain: 2  - Url: 7  - Hash: 17  - IP: 6  - Path: 1  💽Software: WinHTTP, Windows Security, Telegram, Discord, Steam, Outlook, curl, nginx  🔢Algorithms: ecdh, sha256, ecdsa, base64, xor, aes-256-gcm, aes-256-cbc, md5, gzip  🔠Functions: GetComputerName, GetUserName, CreateFile, GetTempPath  🗂️Win API: VirtualAlloc, VirtualProtect, LoadLibrary, GetProcAddress, WinHttpSetOption, GdiplusStartup, GetDC, CreateCompatibleBitmap, BitBlt, CreateProcess, ...  ⚙️Win Services: bits  📜Programming Languages: visual_basic, python, golang  💻Platforms: x64  #threatreport: The GhostShell malware campaign, labeled as MB-0009, has been observed targeting Ukraine's UAV operations and defense supply chain since February 2026. This new threat actor has not been correlated with previously known groups, differentiating its activities through a specific attack infrastructure and methodology. The malware exploits vulnerabilities CVE-2025-8088 and CVE-2025-6218 to deliver malicious payloads disguised in a RAR archive named “Besomar_documentation.rar,” which mimics legitimate documentation associated with the Ukrainian drone manufacturer Besomar. The targeted entities include military units and various defense-sector personnel, indicating a focus on operational access and supply chain intelligence.  The primary components of the malware's architecture involve a multi-stage infection process. The RAR archive drops a Visual Basic Script (VBS) file into the Windows Startup folder, ensuring persistence through the use of relative path traversal. This VBS file subsequently downloads additional executables—122.exe and update.exe—from a command and control (C2) domain, cloudaxis.cc. The behavior of these payloads points to sophisticated evasion techniques, including checks for sandboxes and the use of mutual TLS (mTLS) for secure communication with the C2 server, which only responds to clients that present a valid client certificate.  The executable 122.exe functions as a loader utilizing a CRPT XOR overlay mechanism, capable of executing a second-stage implant directly in memory. The second-stage implant authenticates via an embedded elliptic-curve mTLS client certificate, highlighting the sophisticated use of cryptography within the attack. Conversely, update.exe acts as an in-memory loader that masquerades as a Windows service while performing anti-analysis checks and fetching payloads from the C2 infrastructure. This loader retrieves subsequent shellcode and executes it in memory, effectively evading traditional detection mechanisms.  Additionally, another component, 22.exe, has been identified within this operation. It is characterized as a multi-stage launcher that utilizes AES-256-GCM encryption for configuration parameters and operates as a covert transport and proxy layer using an embedded Xray Core client. More significantly, it delivers Vidar v2, a well-known information stealer, which targets a range of sensitive user information—browser passwords, cookies, and cryptocurrency-related data—via the established proxy tunnel.  The overall structure of this malware campaign demonstrates a strategic approach to targeting high-value supply chain vulnerabilities critical to Ukraine's defense capabilities. With its emphasis on covert operation and data exfiltration, GhostShell poses a significant threat, especially given its potential connections to the geopolitical landscape surrounding the Ukraine conflict. The reported use of Telegram for C2 host resolution further illustrates the flexibility and adaptability of modern cyber threat actor methodologies, complicating traditional attribution efforts, though the presence of specific identifiers, such as the self-named "GhostShell Implant CA," could provide future avenues for analysis and detection.

#threatreport #LowCompleteness PixelSmash – Critical FFmpeg Vulnerability Turns Media Files into Weapons | 23-06-2026 Source: jfrog.com/blog/pixelsmash-cr… Key details below ↓  💀Threats: Pixelsmash_vuln, Supply_chain_technique, Lumma_stealer,  🎯Victims: Media processing applications, Media servers, Cloud storage platforms, Cloud transcoding services, Chat platforms, Network attached storage appliances, Smart televisions, Photo management platforms, Artificial intelligence and machine learning infrastructure, Linux desktop environments, ...  🏭Industry: Iot, Media  🔓CVEs: CVE-2026-8461  \[[Vulners](vulners.com/cve/CVE-2026-846…)]  - CVSS V3.1: *8.8*,   - Vulners: Exploitation: Unknown  📚TTPs: ⚔️Tactics: 2 🛠️Technics: 0  🤖LLM extracted TTPs:` T1036, T1059.004, T1190, T1203, T1204.002, T1499.004  🧨IOCs:  - File: 5  💽Software: MagicYUV, Linux, Jellyfin, Slack, Discord, Telegram, Ubuntu, Debian, Fedora, Alpine, ...  🔠Functions: system, free  #threatreport: A critical vulnerability has been identified in FFmpeg's MagicYUV decoder, designated CVE-2026-8461, which allows for remote code execution through specially crafted media files. This vulnerability results from a heap out-of-bounds write, with a CVSS score of 8.8, and affects numerous applications utilizing FFmpeg, including media processors like Kodi, Jellyfin, and Nextcloud. The issue can be triggered by merely processing a maliciously designed AVI, MKV, or MOV file, leading to crashes of affected applications and, in some cases, to full remote code execution.  To exploit the vulnerability, an attacker must deliver a carefully crafted media file to any software that decodes video using FFmpeg’s libavcodec. This can occur through desktop applications when a user opens a malicious file or when a file is uploaded to a media server, where automatic processing would trigger the vulnerability. Notably, the attack does not require any advanced permissions or user interactions beyond the initial file delivery, making it highly dangerous and exploitable through various means, including torrent downloads that automatically place files in watched directories.  The underlying cause of the vulnerability can be traced back to a rounding mismatch within the MagicYUV decoder's slice handling code. The error lies in improper validation of slice height, allowing attackers to manipulate buffer memory. The implications are serious, resulting not only in application crashes but potentially in arbitrary command execution, demonstrated through successful exploits on Jellyfin, where an attacker gained execution rights through normal media library scanning routines.  The impact of PixelSmash extends widely due to FFmpeg's pervasive integration into applications across the software ecosystem, making it a supply chain vulnerability. Since FFmpeg's libavcodec is a core dependency for numerous projects, many developers do not conduct thorough audits of its codec implementations, leading to silent propagation of this critical flaw into various downstream applications.  Real-world exploitation scenarios also illustrate the ease with which attackers can leverage the vulnerability. The automatic metadata extraction during media uploads to services like Nextcloud and Jellyfin, combined with how damage is executed without alerting administrators, poses significant operational risks. Systems running ongoing FFmpeg services could remain compromised without indication, allowing for potential cost-inefficient exploitation in cloud environments due to the nature of the attack.  Additionally, new attack surfaces emerge in AI/ML infrastructures that process video inputs, suggesting further research into similar vulnerabilities in systems employing libavcodec for untrusted video data. It is imperative for systems that rely on FFmpeg to promptly update to patched versions or disable the vulnerable MagicYUV decoder to mitigate associated risks. This incident highlights the necessity for organizations to scrutinize their software supply chains for vulnerabilities lurking within dependencies, which can manifest severe security ramifications without direct developer involvement.

#threatreport #HighCompleteness The Invisible Stinger: Over 4,000 Outdated Routers Compromised by AryStinger, Turned into Launchpads for Hackers’ Global Attacks | 17-06-2026 Source: blog.xlab.qianxin.com/arysti… Key details below ↓  💀Threats: Arystinger, Dropbear_tool, Gs-netcat, Fscan_tool, Dropbearkey_tool, Masscan_tool,  🎯Victims: Router devices, Network attached storage devices  🏭Industry: Iot  🌐Geo: South korea, Singapore, China, Malaysia, Sweden  🔓CVEs: CVE-2025-11837  \[[Vulners](vulners.com/cve/CVE-2025-118…)]  - CVSS V3.1: *9.8*,   - Vulners: Exploitation: Unknown     Soft:  - qnap malware_remover (<6.6.8.20251023)  CVE-2016-5681  \[[Vulners](vulners.com/cve/CVE-2016-568…)]  - CVSS V3.1: *9.8*,   - Vulners: Exploitation: Unknown     Soft:  - dlink dir-868l_firmware (le2.03)  CVE-2013-3307  \[[Vulners](vulners.com/cve/CVE-2013-330…)]  - CVSS V3.1: *8.3*,   - Vulners: Exploitation: True  🤖LLM extracted TTPs:` T1001, T1016, T1018, T1021.004, T1041, T1046, T1057, T1059.004, T1059.006, T1071.001, ...  🧨IOCs:  - File: 1  - Domain: 1  - Url: 3  - Hash: 52  - IP: 1  💽Software: hat offi, eChat of, curl  🔢Algorithms: xor, md5, gzip  📜Programming Languages: python, java  💻Platforms: mips  #threatreport: The AryStinger malware family exploits vulnerabilities in outdated routers, primarily those using RTL819X series chips from Linksys and D-Link. These routers, which have not received firmware updates or security maintenance, serve as compromised entry points for cyberattacks aimed at reconnaissance and data exfiltration. Specifically, the malware targets two notable vulnerabilities: CVE-2013-3307 and CVE-2016-5681, both of which date back over a decade. AryStinger operates in two versions: the RTL819X version, implemented in C, and the Standard version, implemented in Go, which is designed for NAS devices.  The malware communicates with its command-and-control (C2) server using HTTP/HTTPS, encrypting network traffic with Protobuf and XOR encoding. Both versions support a range of malicious capabilities, including network scanning, service identification, and the establishment of persistent remote management channels via tools like Dropbear and gs-netcat. Infected devices can not only maintain covert control for attackers but also serve as platforms for launching further attacks, such as distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) operations or deploying additional malicious payloads.  Each instance of AryStinger is referred to as an "Executor," allowing attackers to divide tasks and execute reconnaissance activities in parallel, increasing the efficacy of their operations. The malware has been detected in over 4,300 routers worldwide, with significant concentrations in South Korea, China, and Sweden. Its low detection rate in mainstream security products poses a heightened risk, making it difficult to trace the origins of attacks emanating from these compromised devices.  The operation of AryStinger includes various core tasks, such as authentication with the C2 server, downloading additional malware, and executing scanning tasks. The malware enables the collection of sensitive information via network reconnaissance, and potential harmful activities like DNS hijacking for redirecting users to malicious sites.  Furthermore, AryStinger's Standard edition has been enhanced with additional functionality, such as the ability to perform internal network scans and execute dynamic payloads, which increases its versatility and the likelihood of success in target environments. This evolution in malware capabilities significantly raises concerns regarding the integrity and security of networks utilizing outdated hardware, emphasizing the need for continued vigilance and proactive measures in cybersecurity practices.

‼️ The Coca-Cola Company has filed form 8-K due to a cybersecurity incident on behalf of Fairlife, LLC.  "On July 16, 2026, The Coca-Cola Company (the “Company”) announced that fairlife, LLC (“fairlife”), a dairy company owned by the Company, identified unauthorized access by a third party to a portion of its systems, including its production-related systems, in connection with a ransomware event.  After detecting the issue, the Company promptly activated its incident response and business continuity protocols. The Company’s investigation and assessment of the impact of the incident is ongoing, with the assistance of outside advisors and cybersecurity experts. The Company has also notified law enforcement.  Product quality and safety have not been impacted. However, as a result of the incident, production operations at fairlife in the United States are temporarily suspended. fairlife’s Canada production operations are not currently impacted.  The Company is working diligently to complete the investigation and restore the systems and impacted operations. The full scope, nature and impacts of the incident are not yet known. Accordingly, the Company has not yet determined whether the incident is reasonably likely to materially affect the Company.  The information in this Form 8-K shall not be deemed “filed” for purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, nor shall it be deemed incorporated by reference in any filing under the Securities Act of 1933, except as shall be expressly set forth by specific reference in such filing."

According to a post on an underground forum, the group alleges it gained access to the company’s workforce URL and is now exposing sensitive internal data after failing to receive a ransom payment.  The actor alleges that the leak includes a wide range of sensitive employee data and corporate intellectual property. The post enumerates the following categories of exposed information:  *   **Government & Tax Identifiers:** Digital copies of Indonesian National ID Cards (KTP), Family Cards (KK), electronic tax cards (NPWP-Elektronik), salary declarations, and academic certificates. *   **Corporate Payroll & Financial Logs:** Expense ledger spreadsheets, cash advance records, administrative transaction logs, and medical benefits forms for the Indonesian healthcare system (BPJS Kesehatan) and pension records. *   **Talent Tracking & Strategy:** Executive talent cards for specific employees (Fitri Rahmiyani, Anton, and Ilyasa Deva Wirabuana), performance appraisals, and proprietary Balanced Scorecard alignment metrics. *   **Internal HR Guides:** Internal toolkits for Performance Improvement Plans, exit checklists for various sectors (Refinery, Upstream, Corporate, Trading), and the 2023 SMAF Learning Catalogue. *   **Workday Infrastructure:** Archives of Workday Knowledge bases, platform layout blueprints, corporate letterheads, and IT security procedures for handling locked user accounts.  Sinar Mas Agribusiness and Food is one of the world’s largest producers of palm oil, supplying major global brands including Unilever, Nestlé, Procter & Gamble, Mondelēz International, and Johnson & Johnson. The threat actor claims that due to supply chain interdependencies, this breach also impacts these downstream partners, although no evidence of direct compromise at those companies was provided in the post.  The data dump is reportedly hosted on MEGA, with mirrors on transfer.it and anonfilesnew.com, totaling 375.66MB. The actor states that the company was aware of the breach but failed to contact them, leading to the public exposure. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by security researchers or confirmed by Sinar Mas Group.

🚨 Threat Actor Promotes "DarkMatter" Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) Affiliate Program  A threat actor announced the launch of a Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) affiliate program named DarkMatter, targeting small, medium, and large enterprises. According to the post, the group states that it does not permit attacks against organizations located in the CIS region or the healthcare sector, while promoting long-term partnerships and operational support for affiliates.  According to the advertisement, the threat actor provides affiliates with customized ransomware builds, dedicated Tor-based management panels, real-time callback reporting, encrypted communications, and ongoing operational support. The offering also supports multiple payload formats, including executable files, DLLs, PowerShell scripts, batch files, and memory-only (fileless) payloads, allowing affiliates to tailor deployments to different attack scenarios.  The advertised ransomware is claimed to support a wide range of capabilities, including:  - Configurable file encryption and deployment options - Active Directory integration and domain-wide propagation - Network spreading through SMB, PsExec, and Group Policy - Credential dumping and privilege impersonation - Defense evasion and anti-analysis techniques - Anti-debugging, anti-VM, and sandbox detection - Backup and shadow copy deletion - ESXi virtualization environment targeting - Tor-based victim portals for ransom negotiations  #RaaS #ThreatIntelligence #DarkWeb

⚠️ Alleged TotalEnergies Customer Database Leak on Darkweb Forum  A post on the Darkforums underground community claims to offer a database containing personal and account details of 20 million TotalEnergies customers for sale. The actor, operating under the handle @MrL33tH4, alleges that the dataset includes full information for residential clients in France.  According to the listing, the database is priced at 650 euros. The seller provides a sample of the data structure, which reportedly contains the following fields:  *   Civilité (Title) *   Nom (Last Name) *   Prénom (First Name) *   Téléphone (Phone Number) *   Identifiant du point (Point Identifier) *   fournisseur (Supplier) *   Catégorie client (Client Category) *   adresse (Address) *   Code postal (Postal Code) *   Commune (City/Municipality) *   Formule tarifaire (Tariff Formula) *   Calendrier Fournisseur (Supplier Calendar) *   Puissance souscrite demandée (Subscribed Power Requested) *   country  The sample entries shown in the thread display French residential addresses, phone numbers, and specific energy consumption details, such as "Tarif BT <= 36kVA" and subscribed power levels like "6 kVA" or "9 kVA". The actor claims the source website is totalenergies.fr.  TotalEnergies is a major multinational energy company headquartered in France, serving millions of customers globally. If authentic, the exposure of such detailed customer records including names, addresses, and phone numbers could facilitate targeted phishing campaigns, social engineering attacks, or identity theft against French residents. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by TotalEnergies or security researchers.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims 40,000 Askari Bank Records Leaked on Darkweb  A darkweb forum post claims that a database containing approximately 40,000 records from Askari Bank, one of Pakistan’s largest financial institutions, has been compromised and is available for sale. The actor alleges that the leak includes sensitive customer data such as national identification numbers (CNIC), full names, residential addresses, account numbers, and contact details.  According to the listing, the exposed data fields include: *   Bank name and branch name *   CNIC / National ID *   Customer Name *   Address *   Account number *   Contact Number *   Account Type (e.g., Saving A/C, Current A/C)  The post provides sample entries showing customers from branches in Rawalpindi and Karachi, including specific street addresses and phone numbers. Askari Bank is a major player in Pakistan’s banking sector, with a significant retail and corporate customer base. If authentic, the exposure of CNICs alongside account numbers could facilitate targeted phishing campaigns, identity theft, and social engineering attacks against the bank's customers.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by Askari Bank or third-party security researchers.

sample_tweets.text: Critical Oracle E-Business Suite flaw is under active exploitation  CISA confirmed active exploitation of CVE-2026-46817, a critical Oracle E-Business Suite vulnerability affecting the Oracle Payments File Transmission component. The flaw allows an unauthenticated attacker with HTTP access to compromise Oracle Payments and potentially take over vulnerable systems. More than 1,000 internet-exposed Oracle EBS instances were being tracked, although the number vulnerable or already compromised is unknown. U.S. federal agencies were ordered to remediate by July 18, 2026.    #DDW #Vulnerability #Exploitation #oracle

🇧🇷 São Paulo State Education Platform Data Allegedly Offered for Sale  A threat actor claims to be selling more than 4 million student records allegedly extracted from the official "Sala do Futuro" platform operated by the São Paulo State Department of Education (SEDUC-SP).  * The actor claims to possess 4,068,441 student records * The data was allegedly extracted directly from an API * Claimed fields include student IDs, full names, CPF/RG numbers, dates of birth, gender, email addresses, parents' names, home addresses, geolocation coordinates, and class enrollment information * The listing claims the dataset covers schools across the State of São Paulo * At the time of writing, there is no public confirmation from SEDUC-SP regarding the alleged breach  Analyst Note: If authentic, the exposure of education records containing national ID numbers, family information, and precise location data would present significant privacy and identity theft risks. Such datasets are also frequently abused in phishing, fraud, and social engineering campaigns targeting students, parents, and educational institutions.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Brazil

🇭🇳 Threat Actor Claims Sale of 70,000 Payment Cards from Alleged SQL Injection Attack  A threat actor is advertising the sale of approximately 70,000 payment card records, claiming they were obtained by exploiting an SQL injection vulnerability on a Honduran company's website.  * The actor claims the database contains approximately 70,000 payment cards * Allegedly exposed data includes card numbers, cardholder names, expiration dates, and CVV values * The seller claims to have proof of the vulnerability and is offering evidence to serious buyers * The affected organization has not been identified in the advertisement * At the time of writing, there is no public confirmation of the alleged compromise  Analyst Note: If authentic, the presence of full payment card data, including CVV values, would indicate a severe compromise of payment information. Organizations handling cardholder data should ensure PCI DSS compliance, eliminate SQL injection vulnerabilities through secure coding practices, and avoid storing sensitive authentication data such as CVVs after authorization.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Honduras

🇱🇰 Sri Lanka Customs Data Allegedly Leaked — 250,000+ Shipment Records Claimed  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a full export of Sri Lanka Customs shipment manifests, allegedly containing more than 250,000 records and offering additional "live feed" access.  * The actor claims the data was exfiltrated from Sri Lanka Customs internal servers * The advertised dataset allegedly covers approximately 18 months of import activity * Claimed records include bill of lading numbers, container identifiers, shipper and buyer details, logistics information, and invoice-related fields * The post also advertises ongoing access to future shipment data and claims similar datasets from other countries are available upon request * At the time of writing, there is no public confirmation from Sri Lanka Customs regarding these claims  Analyst Note: If authentic, customs manifest data could expose sensitive supply chain relationships, shipment movements, and commercial trade intelligence. Such information could be leveraged for cargo theft, supply chain targeting, corporate espionage, or highly tailored phishing campaigns against logistics and import/export organizations.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #SriLanka

🇮🇩 PNB (Bali State Polytechnic) Student Data Allegedly Leaked  A threat actor has claimed to have leaked student records belonging to PNB (Politeknik Negeri Bali), affecting data from the 2023–2025 academic years.  * The dataset allegedly contains student information from 2023–2025 * The sample shared by the actor includes student names, registration numbers, email addresses, and WhatsApp numbers * Additional fields may be present in the full dataset, but this has not been independently verified * At the time of writing, there is no public confirmation from PNB regarding the alleged breach  Analyst Note: Educational institutions remain attractive targets due to the large volumes of personally identifiable information (PII) they maintain. Even limited student datasets can enable phishing, identity fraud, and social engineering campaigns targeting students and staff.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Indonesia

⚠️ Accor Allegedly Breached — 162,000+ Customer Records Offered for Sale  A threat actor claims to have compromised Accor, the France-based global hospitality group, after gaining access to an employee account.  * The actor allegedly accessed an internal Accor tool named “LUKE” * 162,437 customer records were reportedly collected * The exposed sample allegedly contains customer-related information * The actor claims access to Accor’s internal network, corporate VPN, and additional systems * According to the post, Accor detected the scraping activity and terminated the session before a larger extraction could be completed  The claim is currently based on the threat actor’s forum post and sample data. We have not identified a public confirmation from Accor.  Analyst Note: The actor’s own statement suggests that internal monitoring detected the abnormal activity. However, a compromised employee identity allegedly provided access beyond a single application, highlighting the risks of excessive privileges and insufficient identity segmentation.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Accor

🇺🇸 Massive U.S. Job Seeker Database Allegedly Offered for Sale on Dark Web  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a database containing information on 16 million U.S. job seekers, including approximately 11 million original resumes totaling around 1 TB of data.  According to the listing, the dataset allegedly spans more than 20 years and contains personally identifiable information (PII) along with original resume documents. The actor claims both historical and recent records are included, although there is currently insufficient evidence to independently verify the authenticity, source, or completeness of the data.  The alleged dataset includes: * Full names * Email addresses * Phone numbers * Street addresses * City, state, and ZIP code * Resume metadata * Original resume filenames * Original resume documents (PDF/DOC/DOCX) * Registration and update timestamps  At the time of writing, no organization has publicly confirmed ownership of the alleged dataset or the claimed compromise.  * Country: United States 🇺🇸 * Alleged records: 16 million * Alleged data size: ~1 TB * Status: Unverified claim  Analyst Note: Large collections of resume data are highly valuable to threat actors. Beyond identity theft, such datasets enable highly convincing spear-phishing campaigns, recruitment scams, business email compromise, and AI-powered social engineering by providing extensive professional and personal context on victims.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #USA

🚨 Elon Musk Says X Will Become Fully Open Source  Elon Musk announced that X plans to release its entire platform codebase as open source after completing a security review.  * The release is intended to cover the entire X codebase. * Independent third-party reviewers will be invited to verify that the published code matches the production system. * No release date has been announced. * The plan remains subject to completion of the security review.  Analyst Note: If implemented as described, this would be one of the largest open-source releases ever by a major social media platform. However, publishing source code does not necessarily mean releasing AI models, proprietary datasets, or other sensitive infrastructure components.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #OpenSource

🇦🇷 Argentine Government Credentials Allegedly Offered for Sale on Dark Web  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a bundle of active login credentials for 21 Argentine government platforms.  According to the listing, the package allegedly includes verified access to multiple government portals spanning public administration, judicial services, taxation, municipal systems, and internal communications. The actor claims the credentials are active and ready for use, though no independent evidence has been provided to verify these assertions.  Some of the referenced platforms include: * AFIP (Tax Authority) * PJN SSO (Judiciary) * DNRPA * RRHH GBA * Municipalidad de Neuquén * Webmail services * Additional provincial and municipal government portals  At the time of writing, there is no public confirmation from Argentine authorities that these credentials are genuine or that any of the listed systems have been compromised.  * Country: Argentina 🇦🇷 * Alleged targets: 21 government platforms * Alleged access: Active government login credentials * Status: Unverified claim  Analyst Note: If authentic, access to legitimate government accounts poses a significantly higher risk than leaked databases, potentially enabling unauthorized access to sensitive information, internal communications, and administrative systems. Organizations should immediately review authentication logs, reset affected credentials, and enforce phishing-resistant MFA where possible.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Argentina

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-07-17 19:01 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 보안 위협은 AI를 활용한 공격 자동화와 공급망 공격의 고도화가 두드러지며, 특히 LLM을 악용한 피싱 메일 작성 및 코드 취약점 탐색이 활발해지고 있습니다([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/threat-intelligence/1m-emails-hidden-text-dupe-ai-security-filters)). 또한, 공격자들은 기존의 기술적 취약점 공격보다 사용자의 부주의를 유도하는 게임 파일 위장이나 피싱 메일 등 사회공학적 기법을 선호하는 경향이 뚜렷합니다([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/threatsday-game-cheat-spyware-24-hour-ransomware-chrome-sync-stalking-12-more-stories)). 더불어, CISA는 KEV(Known Exploited Vulnerabilities) 목록을 통해 알려진 취약점에 대한 즉각적인 패치를 강제하고 있으며, 기업들은 AI 거버넌스 부재로 인한 보안 공백을 메우기 위한 전략적 대응이 요구되는 시점입니다([Crowdstrike Blog](https://www.crowdstrike.com/en-us/blog/why-ai-governance-without-guardrails-is-theater/)).

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Cavern Manticore**: 이란 연계 APT 그룹으로, 모듈형 C2 프레임워크를 사용하여 이스라엘 등 주요 타겟을 공격([Check Point Research](https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/cavern-manticore-exposing-iran-linked-modular-c2-framework/)).
*   **GoSerpent**: 동남아시아 정부 기관을 타겟으로 하는 백도어 캠페인으로, Stowaway RAT 등을 활용해 데이터 탈취 수행([Securelist](https://securelist.com/goserpent-backdoor-in-southeast-asia/120687/)).
*   **OkoBot**: 암호화폐 사용자를 타겟으로 하는 악성코드 프레임웨어로, 브라우저 모니터링 및 시드 구문 탈취([Securelist](https://securelist.com/okobot-framework-targets-cryptocurrency-wallets/120660/)).
*   **Scattered Spider**: 2024년 Transport for London 해킹 사건의 주범으로, 최근 5.5년형 선고([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/two-scattered-spider-hackers-get-55.html)).

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **이스트시큐리티 ESRC**: 국세청 사칭 피싱 메일을 통한 원격제어 악성코드 유포 주의보 발령([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5771)).
*   **공급망 공격**: Chai.js 플러그인으로 위장한 북한발 npm 악성 패키지 'chai-as-init' 유포 확인([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5766)).
*   **오픈소스 서밋 코리아**: 8월 13일~14일 서울 개최 예정, AI 및 보안 기술 공유([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207655)).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-58644**: Microsoft SharePoint Server RCE, 심각도 Critical (CVSS 9.8), CISA KEV 등록 및 활발히 악용 중([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/cisa-adds-exploited-sharepoint-rce-zero.html)).
*   **CVE-2026-42533**: NGINX RCE, 심각도 Critical (CVSS 9.2), 원격 코드 실행 가능([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207657)).

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **CVE**: 
    *   CVE-2026-58644 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/cisa-adds-exploited-sharepoint-rce-zero.html))
    *   CVE-2026-42533 ([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207657))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **SharePoint 패치**: CVE-2026-58644 취약점에 대해 CISA 권고에 따라 즉각적인 보안 업데이트 적용.
*   **NGINX 점검**: 인터넷에 노출된 NGINX 서버의 버전을 확인하고 CVE-2026-42533 패치 적용.
*   **공급망 보안**: npm 등 오픈소스 패키지 사용 시 출처가 불분명한 플러그인(예: chai-as-init) 사용을 지양하고 의존성 검사 강화.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수**: 8/10
*   **근거**: CISA KEV에 등록된 SharePoint RCE 취약점이 활발히 악용되고 있으며, 공급망 공격 및 AI를 활용한 피싱 위협이 지속적으로 증가하고 있어 기업 보안 환경의 위험도가 높음.

📊 Gemini 토큰: 이번 14,682개 (입력 13,366 / 출력 1,316)
📈 세션 누적: 14,682 / 1,000,000 (1.5%) | 세션 잔여 추정 985,318개

news_articles.title: 리눅스재단, '오픈소스 서밋 코리아' 개최...AI·클라우드·보안 등 최신 기술 공유
news_articles.summary: 리눅스재단(The Linux Foundation)이 오는 8월 13일부터 14일까지 서울 그랜드 인터컨티넨탈 파르나스 호텔에서 '오픈소스 서밋 코리아(Open Source Summit Korea)'를 개최한다.이번 행사는 오픈소스 기술을 매개로 인공지능(AI), 리눅스, 클라우드 인프라, 통신, 임베디드 시스템 등 최신 컴퓨팅 분야의 개발자와 엔지니어들이 모여 기술 협력 방안을 논의하는 자리다.올해 행사에는 전 세계 12개국에서 약 800명의 전문가가 참석할 예정이다. 리눅스재단은 기조연설 8개를 비롯해 기술 세션 68개, 전문가

news_articles.title: Ransom & Dark Web Issues 2026년 7월 3주차
news_articles.summary: ASEC Blog를 통해 한 주간의 ‘Ransom & Dark Web Issues’ – 2026년 7월 3주차를 게시한다.           DragonForce, 사우디아라비아 화학 제조 기업 대상 랜섬웨어 공격 AiLock, 일본 최대 택시 및 리무진 운영 기업 대상 랜섬웨어 공격 일본 최대 냉동 식품 기업의 사이버 공격 피해, 식품 공급망 전반 서비스 중단

news_articles.title: 전파 악성코드를 악용한 리눅스 SSH 서버 대상 코인 마이너 유포 사례
news_articles.summary: AhnLab SEcurity intelligence Center(ASEC)은 다수의 허니팟을 활용해 부적절하게 관리되고 있는 리눅스 서버를 대상으로 한 공격을 모니터링하고 있다. 최근에는 전파 기능을 수행하는 악성코드를 통해 XMRig 코인 마이너를 설치하는 사례를 확인하였다.   공격 사례에서는 ShellBot 악성코드뿐만 아니라 MIG LogCleaner, XHide 같은 도구들이 사용되었다. 공격자는 Go 언어로 제작된 다운로더 및 전파 악성코드를 제작해 사용하였으며 이외에도 Shc(Shell Script Compil

news_articles.title: 2026년 6월 인포스틸러 동향 보고서
news_articles.summary: 내용 이 보고서는 2026년 6월 동안 수집된 신규 인포스틸러의 유포 채널, 악성코드 수량, 탐지 수량, 위장 대상 기업 정보를 정리한다. 수집 샘플은 ASEC(AhnLab SEcurity intelligence Center)에서 운영하는 자동 수집 시스템, 이메일 허니팟 시스템, 악성코드 C2 자동 분석 시스템을 통해 확보되었다. 목적 및 범위 보고서는 인포스틸러 악성코드의 유포 수량, 위장 기법, 유포 방식의 추이를 확인하는 용도로 […]
